restaurants.name: L'angolo
restaurants.description: The husband studied cooking in Nice, the wife in Tuscany. Today the two of them express simple, honest home cooking and regional dishes using Japanese ingredients. Pairings courses combine food with organic wines favoured by the couple. Working in shifts, the wife serves Italian cuisine at lunchtime, while the husband provides cuisine based on French cooking for dinner, each making the most of their area of expertise.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天神橋1-12-1

restaurants.name: LACERBA
restaurants.address: 2F, Shin Daibiru, 1-2-1 Dojimahama, Kita-ku, Osaka, 530-0004, Japan
restaurants.description: Masaaki Fujita reaches deep into the character and history of Japan and Italy to conjure his cuisine. Eel, Chinese yam and red rice on a single plate fuse the gustatory senses of the two countries, both rooted deeply in cultures of river fish and rice. Zest of Japanese pepper married with the bittersweet of red wine sauce is somehow nostalgic. Fujita’s motto is ‘to be master of one is to be master of all’. His long study of hometown cooking followed by his own innovations proves the point.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区堂島浜1-2-1 2F, Shin Daibiru

restaurants.name: LE PONT DE CIEL
restaurants.address: B1F, Nihonseimei Yodoyabashi Bldg, 3-5-29 Kitahama, Chuo-ku, Osaka, 540-0031, Japan
restaurants.description: To mark its half-century anniversary, LE PONT DE CIEL broke new ground, converting to a ‘counter French’ format. The open kitchen buzzes with activity as cooks and serving staff attend customers. The key to the fare here is cooking with firewood. Vegetables are roasted, fish smoked, meat wrapped in flame. The name means ‘bridge across the sky’, reflecting the chef’s desire to foster a culture that connects a relaxing time with delicious cuisine.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区北浜東3-5-29 B1F, Nihonseimei Yodoyabashi Bldg

restaurants.name: LES SOUVENIRS
restaurants.description: The chef layers Japanese elements over French cuisine. Insisting on only the finest Japan-grown ingredients in season, he uses kombu dashi and soy sauce as subtle accents of flavour. The chef values knowing the people behind his produce, and deepens his ties with them, while crockery and cutlery are specially commissioned from artisans. The restaurant name is French for ‘memories’, reflecting the chef’s desire to make it a place that recalls the happiness of chatting contently around a dining table.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区曽根崎新地1-2-10 5F

restaurants.name: LOUISE
restaurants.description: The Cameroon-born chef co-stars with the hospitable sommelier. What prompted a chef who pursued gastronomy in Paris to set up shop in Osaka. Simply put, a fascination with the foodstuffs of Japan. Layered ingredients, effectively seasoned with herbs and spices, bring out a distinctive character. The counter, where you can chat with staff and enjoy the delicious aromas wafting from the kitchen, offers the best seats in the house.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市西区立売堀1-1-5

restaurants.name: La Baie
restaurants.address: 5F, The Ritz-Carlton, Osaka, 2-5-25 Umeda, Kita-ku, Osaka, 530-0001, Japan
restaurants.description: A dining room fit for a noble mansion, décor of stunning symmetry and graceful service by the waitstaff create a moment cocooned in splendour. Commanding the kitchen is Christophe Gibert, a native of Bretagne. Accustomed to life by the sea, Gibert makes extensive use of seaweed. His preparations, with their strong accent on sauces, convey his spirit of respect for classic cooking.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区梅田2-5-25 5F, The Ritz-Carlton, Osaka

restaurants.name: La Bécasse
restaurants.description: La Bécasse aspires to greet guests with something new and exciting every time they visit. Rather than follow past recipes, the restaurant enlivens familiar ingredients with creative preparation techniques and sauces. A sojourn in France taught the chef the importance of terroir, the characteristics of the land from which sprouts the character of each ingredient. To convey the blessings of each season, the chef visits markets daily, seeking new ways to reflect Japanese terroir through French cooking.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区平野町3-3-9

restaurants.name: La Cime
restaurants.description: Yusuke Takada unravels the mysteries of the classics while pursuing the modern. Takada is the author of a new French cuisine, one imbued with the essence of Japan. Takada’s aim in using traditional ingredients such as kudzu vine, tofu skin, miso and wasabi is to proclaim the goodness of foodstuffs grown and raised in Japan. Cultural elements from Osaka and his native Amami Ōshima weave together into a story that is unique and fascinating.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区瓦町3-2-15

restaurants.name: La Kanro
restaurants.description: Presentation sparkles with colour here as the chef skilfully weaves in flowers and herbs and decorates his plates with sauces of vegetable and spices. Combo platters of appetisers, garnished with flowers, beguile the senses. Trying out a departure from normal French practice, meat dishes are stripped of garnishes. Prix fixe menus change monthly and the chef repeatedly updates his menus.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天神西町3-9

restaurants.name: La Lucciola
restaurants.description: A small light shines from a back alley. The restaurant’s name means ‘firefly’, and the chef’s sunny smile lights up the spirits of his guests. The fare is Italian, yet Japanese sensibility lives within it. Counter seating is kappo-influenced, while the charcoal grill reflects use of longstanding Japanese methods. Japanese and Western serving-ware are used to suit each dish; the chef personally repairing pottery with kintsugi gold and lacquer comes from the Japanese spirit. Many customers frequent La Lucciola to enjoy the presence of the good-humoured chef.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市福島区福島5-7-3

restaurants.name: La bonne tâche
restaurants.description: Red bell-pepper mousse, smoked salmon, roast duck; ever since it opened, the fare here has been simple, out of respect for the ingredients. The chef seeks to draw closer to perfection every day, advancing in his craft. Cooking skill, recipes and spirituality are all things he learned as an apprentice. Cast your eye around the kitchen and notice how all the pots and pans are gleaming. Respect for the tools of his trade drive the chef to keep honing his technique.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-1-8

restaurants.name: Le Caneton
restaurants.description: The name is French for ‘duckling’, reflecting the chef’s passionate pursuit of duck and foie gras cuisine. He places the accent on prix fixe, the better to ensure perfection in each dish. Playful amuse-bouche are dramatically presented on a high-tea stand. For the main course, Osaka duck is naturally de rigueur. His wife, the pâtissière of the establishment, flexes her talents in the dessert department.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区上本町西2-6-23

restaurants.name: Le Nez
restaurants.description: Not surprisingly for a restaurant whose name means ‘the nose’, aroma is the unifying theme here. The house is redolent of ingredients in season and the fragrances of fresh cooking. An amuse-gueule named ‘Hakoniwa’ is, as the name suggests, like a miniature seasonal garden in a little box. Scattering the blessings of Wakayama, Le Nez carries the hometown breezes of that prefecture. Nanko plums, for example, are a speciality of the chef’s birthplace of Minabe. The aroma of meat, adroitly grilled over Kishu-binchotan, wafts on the air and beguiles the nose.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区東心斎橋1-3-29 2F

restaurants.name: Le progrès
restaurants.description: The chef and sommelier, who apprenticed at the same restaurant, opened this place together. Their aim is to present French cuisine that conveys Japan’s climate and culture. They pursue originality by applying French technique to Japanese ingredients in season. The menu is written vertically, inspired by kaiseki. Pairings with Japanese tea spring from the house’s Japanese soul. Le Progrès is a restaurant that ‘progresses’ through the passion of its two partners.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区堂島浜2-1-13

restaurants.name: MATO
restaurants.description: ‘Mato’ means a fascinating city that fuses East and West, ancient and modern, and changes almost like magic. MATO was founded by two women with Shanghai heritage, and the name celebrates those roots of that classic ‘mato’. The prix fixe menu features traditional fare from various regions of China, assembled with a balanced diet in mind. Seasonal Japanese ingredients are incorporated in dishes which are given a modern touch. To deepen your knowledge of Chinese culinary culture, ask for the Chinese tea pairings.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天満3-11-7 2F

restaurants.name: Macauda
restaurants.description: Fish dishes are the pride of this Italian restaurant, helmed by a chef who apprenticed at a trattoria that specialised in seafood. As he travelled through the various regions of Italy, he became fond of fish cuisine, going on to work in areas near the sea such as Puglia and Sicily. Today he interprets the rural cuisine and homemade pastas he studied in southern Italy through the marine products of Japan. ‘Macauda’ is the name of the house in which he stayed in Sicily that won his heart.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区常盤町2-1-3

restaurants.name: Man-u
restaurants.description: Man-u’s ingenious oden, which borrows from other Japanese cuisines, is a whirlwind of flavour changes. Items are selected from a large pot and paired with condiments and broth to bring different flavours to the fore. Beef sinew is cooked with green onions; octopus is paired with unripe pepper. Clams, wakame seaweed with bamboo shoots, and pike conger with matsutake mushrooms are served as soup dishes. Lettuce, tomato and figs dressed with starchy dashi soup stock sauce provide interesting alternatives. Painstaking preparation results in an oden with a unique finish.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区堂島1-2-33 5F

restaurants.name: Mashino Ken
restaurants.description: The cuisine of Hong Kong touched off a journey of exploration into the depth and richness of Chinese cooking. The chef studied each region’s history and culture to plumb the origins of each. His passion shines as he explains the roots of each cuisine, referring to his own experiences and the menu in Cantonese. Peking duck as standard fare ensures he never forgets where he came from. Patient work making miso and rolling dough drive his zeal for constant improvement.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区淡路町1-3-6 2F

restaurants.name: Masuda
restaurants.description: Hanging in the kitchen is a framed calligraphy motto bearing the phrase ‘Hakuraku no ikko’ -a reference to distinguishing true quality. It reflects the chef’s wish to keep improving so as to earn the respect of his diners. For the chefs, the plain wood counter is the stage on which they perform as one cohesive unit. Food and dishware captivate with seasonal combinations. The hassun is especially attention-grabbing: beauty sculpted from the bounty of land and sea, where both rich and light coexist. Guests here are welcomed with carefully prepared dishes.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区心斎橋筋1-3-12 2F

restaurants.name: Matsuzushi Tanigawa
restaurants.description: The second-generation owner-chef welcomes you to his shop tucked away in a residential quarter of Abeno. Though the interior is newly renovated to serve as the chef’s own stage, the exterior, passed down from his father, deliberately retains a Showa era look. With roots planted firmly in the local community, the chef takes care to source his fish from Osaka Bay and nearby seas. He rests his fish to draw out the flavour, accenting it again by serving it on mildly vinegared rice. The friendly air of the gentle chef brings back nostalgic memories of the neighbourhood sushi houses of old.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市阿倍野区王子町3-13-6

restaurants.name: Mendokoro Subete
restaurants.description: The deliciousness of chicken works its magic in the soup. ‘Chuka Soba’ is ramen served in a refreshing clear soup; ‘Noko Tori Soba’ is ramen in rich, creamy chicken broth. If you can’t finish your soup, ask for it to be poured over rice with Tsuruhashi kimchi to make rice gruel. The ‘hidden menu’ on the reverse side of the menu catches one’s eye. Together, the front and back menus tell you ‘all about ramen’, which is what the shop’s name means. Savour a bowl from a proprietor who devotes himself whole-heartedly to the craft of ramen.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市生野区桃谷2-17-12

restaurants.name: Michino Le Tourbillon
restaurants.description: Tadashi Michino has been a driving force in Kansai’s French-restaurant community, powered by his avant-garde spirit. Michino ran a restaurant focused on vegetables before his current project, in which he partners with a biologist to explore the future of food. ‘Tourbillon’ means ‘whirlwind’. Being a chef is life itself, he says, and he is determined to always be a fresh wind that stirs up change and moves people’s souls.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市福島区福島6-9-11

restaurants.name: Miyamoto
restaurants.description: The crockery forms an essential part of the cuisine of Miyamoto. From a collection years in the making, each item is carefully selected to complement the item served on it. The arrangements remain simple to let this harmony shine, and there is a refreshing honesty in the chef’s unstinting use of ingredients. He honours the customs of Japanese cuisine yet cooks unbound by theory; he aspires to run a restaurant where he freely creates what is simply delicious. House-made udon is prepared according to the season. The purity of white rice cleanses the soul.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区東天満2-10-28

restaurants.name: Mugito Mensuke
restaurants.description: Queues form daily in front of this popular restaurant. The earthen wall made by a plasterer and counter seating with wooden trays are typical of neighbourhood Japanese restaurants. The dish that brings in many diners is the soy-sauce ramen made with chicken broth; the heart of its flavour is the mixture of guinea fowl and free-range chicken, raised together by a farmer who shares the chef’s vision. The flavours of the two fowl are layered within the clear, amber-coloured soup. House-made noodles, crafted for a satisfying finish, balance and support the character of the bowl.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区豊崎3-4-12

restaurants.name: NELU KORAIBASHI
restaurants.description: The red-brick Western-style building was constructed at the dawn of the 20th century. Restaurants opened one after the other in those days as the winds of modernisation blew through Japan. With its sparkling chandeliers, the dining room recalls the stately home of some European aristocrat. The prix fixe menu is crafted with abundant imagination, each dish telling a story. ‘Nelu’ derives from the Japanese word ‘neru’, meaning to knead or temper, and the first character in the word for ‘brickwork’. The chef mixes innovation with the traditions of French cuisine to create a modern culinary styling.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区高麗橋2-6-4

restaurants.name: Naniwa Okina
restaurants.description: Oimatsu-dori is famed as a street of antique shops and galleries. Celebrating the street’s past as the approach to Osaka Tenmangu Shrine, the restaurant’s noren features the shrine’s plum-flower crest. To preserve Osaka’s dashi culture, the third-generation owner-chef uses his grandfather’s recipe for noodle broth made from kombu and light soy sauce. In contrast, he learned his noodle-making technique under the guidance of a master in Yamanashi Prefecture. The noodles are made using sacred water from the shrine, in gratitude to the area. Truly, this is the soba crafted by a venerable gentleman—an okina of Naniwa.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-1-18

restaurants.name: Naniwakappo NOBORU
restaurants.description: ‘Flavour first’ is the watchword at this kappo-style restaurant, where food is prepared before your eyes. The chef also serves generous helpings of smiles and conversation. Orders are placed in kaiseki fashion but varied by the mood of the day and guest preference. ‘Shinjo o hakari, ninjo wo kurau’— words from the restaurant where the chef apprenticed, serves as an exhortation to be thankful for the good fortune of providing what the diner desires. The craftsman spirit of a Naniwa (Osaka) kappo is enshrined in the name.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区大淀南1-3-12

restaurants.name: Naniwaryori Satou
restaurants.description: The chef honed his skills at a Naniwa (Osaka-style) kappo. The restaurant’s name, ‘Satou’, adds a kanji for the ‘u’ to his surname, Sato, in homage to the shop where he apprenticed. The long Japanese menu is written right-to-left, like a scroll. His speciality, passed down from his mentor along with his cherished serving bowls, is ‘Tenjin Kobai Mushi’, savoury egg custard prepared with dried-plum broth and finished with a delicate plum glaze. The chef prides himself on his quick thinking in picking up guests’ preferences. An heir to the spirit and skills of Osaka cuisine.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区同心1-1-11

restaurants.name: Naniwaryori Yu
restaurants.description: Located close to the beloved Osaka Temmangu Shrine, the ‘Yu’ in the restaurant’s name is a character in the name of the chef’s mentor. Apprenticing at an old-school Osaka kappo, the chef learned not only cooking skills but also the bold spirit of a chef who holds court behind the counter. Courteous yet charming, his gregarious manner keeps the conversation flowing. The format is omakase, but with the flexibility to indulge diners’ whims.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区東天満1-9-17

restaurants.name: Nikomi Kimura
restaurants.description: Home cooking and sake, enfolded in the nostalgic charm of a Japanese-style house. ‘Nikomi’ means ‘stew’, and stews are the chef’s signature dishes. Sukiyaki-style simmered beef and tofu, stewed beef tendon with konnyaku, and chicken meatballs are offered year-round; in winter, oden is added to the menu. ‘Fukusa Tamago’ is a rolled omelette mixed with vegetables. Western favourites such as macaroni salad and gratin can be had as well. The dishes with which the chef displays her talents are simple and full of warmth.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区谷町6-18-6

restaurants.name: Nishideria
restaurants.description: The style is ‘Naniwa Italian’, Italian cuisine that highlights the charms of Osaka. Interpreting Italian cooking techniques through Kansai ingredients creates a cuisine found only here. ‘Zeppole alla Osacano’ combines a Neapolitan pastry of deep-fried dough with an Osaka takoyaki. The restaurant works with farmers to grow the vegetables it uses, adopting a food producer’s point of view. For the chef, this attitude is a way of giving something back to the region that raised him.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区天満橋京町3-8 2F

restaurants.name: Nishino
restaurants.description: The chef’s creed is devotion to his craft heart and soul. Having learned this attitude to cooking from his mentor at Taian, where he apprenticed, he constantly burnishes his culinary skills through meticulous attention to ingredient selection, food preparation methods and use of dishware. One vital contributor to his menus is the charcoal flame. The chef’s experience speaks through his pan-seared sashimi and grilled dishes wreathed in aromatic smoke. Inventiveness and painstaking diligence create cuisine that impresses.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市西区京町堀1-9-21

restaurants.name: Nishishinsaibashi Yuno
restaurants.description: Dreaming up cuisine that matches the food to the guest is the mission of this Naniwa kappo. The menu’s called ‘Yuno’s omakase’ proclaiming a free-and-easy approach that strays from the norms of Japanese cuisine. Sashimi assortments are paired with onion dipping sauce or condiments; grilled items are dressed with vegetable sauces. Fishbone broth used in hot-pot fare attests to the restaurant’s spirit of wasting nothing. Come to enjoy the chef’s playful spirit and flavours of Osaka expressed through quality ingredients.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区西心斎橋1-10-35

restaurants.name: Nishitemma Ichigaya
restaurants.description: Nishitemma Ichigaya upholds the traditions of Osaka while incorporating new influences. Creative inspiration and painstaking attention to detail are the watchwords here. Use of ma-kombu kelp to enliven the broth of soup dishes pays tribute to Semba merchant food culture. Traditional vegetables of Osaka showcase the attractions of that great commercial city. From time to time, French methods are employed as well. The seafood with miso sauce américain is a flavour found only here.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満2-10-9

restaurants.name: Numata
restaurants.description: The tempura fried by the chef as his heart dictates comes out refined. To bring the flavour of carefully selected ingredients, the main oil of choice is Taihaku sesame oil. The coating is infused with bubbles, giving it a light texture. In a distinctive presentation style, seafood and vegetables that work well together are served in alternating pairs. The final dish is the guest’s choice of tendon, tempura over rice; tencha, tendon steeped in roasted green tea; or tenmusu, onigiri filled with shrimp tempura. The chef does not neglect training the next generation, either, entrusting understudies with running his branch restaurants.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区曽根崎新地1-10-2 7F

restaurants.name: Numata Sou
restaurants.description: Tempura is simple: coat food in batter and deep-fry it. Yet from that very simplicity arises the subtlety and difficulty of the craft. Green asparagus spears are plump with flavoursome moisture; drop the spears into hot oil and their moisture quickly heats up, turns to steam and leaps from the fryer. The role of the coating, and the essence of tempura, is to capture that delicious moisture and concentrate it. Experience cultivated through training turns tempura from good to magnificent.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-7-7 3F

restaurants.name: OIMATSU Tempura Suzuki
restaurants.description: The tempura here shows an awareness of the start and end of each ingredient’s season, reflecting the chef’s experience in Japanese cuisine. Thinly battered items are lightly fried in safflower oil to convey their flavour plainly and honestly. Tiger prawn and cod milt are wrapped in perilla leaves for added fragrance. Wagyu fillet is grilled to a rosy pink with consummate skill. Red pickled ginger added to kakiage (all-in-one seafood and vegetable tempura blend) is a unique touch that is pure Osaka.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-4-2

restaurants.name: Ogimachi Udonya Asuro
restaurants.description: Located close to Tenjinbashi-suji, Kansai’s longest shopping street. The shop seems to be in competition for the longest queue to get in. The dish of renown is ‘Toriten to Taichikuwaten to Ontama Bukkake’ (udon topped with chicken tempura, sea bream paste cake tempura and soft-boiled egg). If the pure flavour of dipping sauce and noodles is what you’re after, try the ‘Hiyakake’. The clear sauce and delicious noodles are standouts, so the flavour of the seafood broth and the springiness of the noodles have your undivided attention. Choosing your favourite toppings adds to the fun.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天神橋3-8-3

restaurants.name: Oimatsu Hisano
restaurants.description: The chef interprets the seasons through kaiseki, adding twists and tricks that make the cuisine his own. To express each season, appetisers are accompanied by leaves collected on hill and dale and a slim strip of paper is inscribed with a seasonal phrase. White rice, cooked in clay pots, is specially selected and treated. Rice grown in serpentinite soil, with its rich mineral content, is seasoned with salt and soy sauce to bring out umami and flavour. In a unique touch, scorched rice is served like a rice cracker. The basics of Japanese cuisine are safely guarded, yet imagination has room to run.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-8-3

restaurants.name: Oimatsu Kitagawa
restaurants.description: The chef never forgets his connection to his apprentice days. From his two mentors, he learned the basics of Japanese cuisine and the spirit of delighting diners. Years of experience are reflected in skilful work at counter and grill, convivial conversation, and creative food preparation. The restaurant’s name, Kitagawa, includes characters meaning ‘many’ and ‘joy’. We sense a strange connection between things no one man could accomplish alone.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-12-27

restaurants.name: Oku
restaurants.description: The owner-chef opened a counter restaurant specialising in unagi after studying Japanese cuisine. The theme is ‘unagi and sake’, so the fare consists of the unagi set menu only. The menu uses fresh vegetables to impart seasonal moods. Ingredients are boiled, fried or straw-smoked for a unique sensibility. Unagi on rice brings the meal to a close, delighting the palate with rice freshly steamed in clay pots. The owner-chef’s experience also shines in the endless variety of vessels.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市都島区片町2-1-37

restaurants.name: Ono
restaurants.description: As the official ‘Tourism Ambassador for Awaji’s Food Culture’, Kota Ono employs Awaji ingredients in each item of his omakase set menu, paying homage to the island of his birth. He proudly sources not only seafood and wagyu but also soy sauce and even salt from his native Awaji, expounding its charms from the bustle of Kitashinchi. Luxury foods of the season adorn a large platter, unstintingly served in hearty portions. The goal is to be a place where flavoursome food is served, without forgetting imagination and playfulness.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区曽根崎新地1-2-22 6F

restaurants.name: Oribe
restaurants.description: French bistro and teppanyaki cooking are the draw at Oribe. The impressive range of appetisers includes items inspired by French standards such as paté and smoked salmon. Its famous cabbage steak was dreamed up from the essential ingredients of okonomiyaki. Speaking of okonomiyaki, the meal ends with Osaka’s famous savoury pancake, a popular item here. The restaurant was named for the chef’s penchant for Oribe ware, and this style of pottery inspires the green theme for its décor.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市西区新町1-6-18

restaurants.name: Oryori Amenimomakezu
restaurants.description: ‘Ame ni mo makezu’ (Be not Defeated by the Rain) is a poem by Kenji Miyazawa about caring for others, unbowed by hardship. Feeling deep affinity for this attitude to life, the owner-chef declares through this naming his intention to live for others through his cooking. Having apprenticed at an izakaya run by a liquor shop, the chef stocks an impressive range of sake from all around Japan. Focusing on how well dishes pair with sake, the omakase-only service also uses fermented seasonings. No-nonsense Japanese dishes mixed with bar snacks express the owner-chef’s unaffected personality.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-10-17

restaurants.name: Oryori Horikawa
restaurants.description: Japanese cuisine reflects a spirit of gratitude for the bounty of nature and of prayer for people’s happiness. The chef values that spirit, pouring it into every dish he makes. The menu, woven from annual festivities, unfolds as a tale of the seasons. Skills honed at a ryotei live in the chef’s painstaking preparation and every detail of arrangements. The origins and customary usages of each cup and plate, related by the proprietress, add grace notes to the meal.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天満4-8-17

restaurants.name: Oryori Matsumura
restaurants.description: Tomonori Matsumura built the foundations of his career at a ryotei before travelling to France to add Western techniques to his toolkit. His creed is ‘Make ingredients taste even more like themselves’. For example, shrimp dishes use reduced shrimp stock to enhance the star ingredient. Butter, garlic and fresh cream are sometimes used to lend subtle notes to his seasonings. Matsumura’s aim is to create recipes that will resonate a century from now; Japanese cuisine that will be talked about for generations.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市城東区蒲生4-20-11

restaurants.name: Oryori Nonohara
restaurants.description: The restaurant weaves the culture and traditional ceremonies of Japan into its menu. In the seventh lunar month, to celebrate Tanabata, the Star Festival, wanmono is served in lacquer bowls featuring the Milky Way in powdered precious metal. Other tableware also reflects the seasons. One wanmono item features a pairing of hamo and samatsu mushroom in a dish that recalls the tale of Hikoboshi and Orihime: the stars Altair and Vega, two lovers destined to meet but once a year. Sweets include umegae mochi, from Dazaifu Tenmangu Shrine in Fukuoka. Tea from the Yame area of Fukuoka is served in homage to the chef’s hometown.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市福島区福島7-17-11

restaurants.name: Oryori Yamada
restaurants.description: Great ingenuity is poured into the menu to fill it with creative cuisine. Sashimi is garnished with condiments such as deep-fried onions and coarsely grated daikon. Fish cuisine is accented with sauces of mushrooms and vegetables. As a born-and-bred Osakan, the chef gravitates toward Naniwa’s traditional vegetables. The meal wraps up with three types of rice dishes: white rice and several accompaniments, pickled fish on rice and seasonal mixed rice. The generosity of free refills ensures that guests leave satisfied.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-7-7 4F

restaurants.name: Osteria La Cicerchia
restaurants.description: Kumiko Muraji hopes to serve as a bridge between Osaka and her beloved Marche region of Italy. The town where she stayed is renowned for its cicerchia beans, known in Japanese as renriso. Noting that the character for ‘ren’ is the same as her surname, she felt the hand of destiny. A area of foothills and mountains, Marche is a region distinguished for its vegetables and meats. Majolica pottery adorned with cicerchia flower-and-bean motifs complements the shop’s honest fare.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市西区京町堀2-3-4 3F

restaurants.name: Osteria Ottanta Sette
restaurants.description: Franciacorta is a sparkling wine from Italy’s Lombardy region. The couple who run this osteria used to run one back in Italy and now offer both Lombardy wines and cuisine. Dishes are faithful to tradition: risotto is made with Carnaroli rice; pasta is served al dente. The number ‘87’ in Italian is ‘ottanta sette’. This is a play on the phrase ‘ho tanta sete’, meaning ‘I’m thirsty’. Wet your whistle on some Italian bubbly.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-1-11

restaurants.name: Otsuki
restaurants.description: Do everything to the best of your ability, his mentor taught him. The chef takes those words to heart, channelling all his skills to offer fresh-from-the-grill aromas and flavours. The menu strikes a delightful balance between seasonal fare and creative cuisine. New items are constantly added, rewarding every visit with fresh sensations. Eager to satisfy expectations, the chef caters to diners’ preferences, adjusting portions to focus on customer satisfaction.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市福島区福島1-2-4

restaurants.name: Oudon Yomogi
restaurants.description: This à la carte udon shop is a project of the proprietress of the Japanese restaurant Miyamoto. The plain-wood counter and bamboo accents lend the restaurant a kappo feel. À la carte dining is available for both lunch and dinner, while dinner service generally begins with a set offering of appetiser and hassun. Udon noodles are house-made, their broth light and clear like Japanese soups. Sake and side dishes end with a serving of udon; closing with udon rather than soba is one of the restaurant’s charms.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市福島区鷺洲2-10-6

restaurants.name: P greco
restaurants.description: In Italy, the chef experienced the fierce pride the Italians have in their home regions. Deeply impressed by the unique cuisines of people who produce and consume locally all their lives, he set his sights on combining Japanese sensibilities with Italian cooking. One example is anago carpaccio: deboned conger eel accompanied by tomato and bagna càuda. The focus on seafood derives from his love of the bounty of the sea, learned at his grandmother’s house in Okinoshima.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-1-20

restaurants.name: PITAK GOHAN
restaurants.description: Chef Pitak from Bangkok offers food from his native Thailand. Exotic aromas of herbs, spices and fish sauce thrill our souls. Temptations include salad of ground pork and glass noodles, a Thai-style grilled chicken dressed with coriander known as gai yang, and curry stir-fry with seafood and egg. The generous portions of the à la carte dishes encourage diners to share. The everyday flavours of Thailand live and breathe down this Nakazakicho alley.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区中崎2-2-13

restaurants.name: PONTE VECCHIO
restaurants.description: Daisuke Yamane’s culinary goal is ‘optimal cuisine’. Cooking and composition are kept to a minimum, so that beauty flows from function. Each ingredient serves a purpose. The chef rejects needless decoration, knowing too much can be as bad as too little. Disparate elements judiciously balanced create harmony—for example, the luxury of caviar meeting the familiarity of potato.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区北浜1-8-16

restaurants.name: PRESQU'ÎLE
restaurants.address: 2F, Yodoyabashi odona, 4-1-1 Imabashi, Chuo-ku, Osaka, 541-0042, Japan
restaurants.description: PRESQU'ÎLE takes its name from a district of Lyons, reflecting the chef’s respect for the place where he trained and his resolve to carry its traditions forward for the next generation. Classical technique underpins French cooking in which sauces play a central role. A speciality is duck and foie gras tourte served with sauce poured tableside. Fruit displayed on the table may also appear as crêpes suzette.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区今橋4-1-1 2F, Yodoyabashi odona

restaurants.name: PRESTAU
restaurants.description: The restaurant’s name is a portmanteau of ‘impress’ combined with ‘restaurant’. The concept is a ‘restaurant that touches the heartstrings’, and PRESTAU aims to do just that. His experience in Kobe leads the chef to favour ingredients from Hyogo Prefecture, served on Tambayaki flatware. Preparing each item to its best advantage is his top priority. This is Italian cuisine in a modern style.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市西区新町2-17-17

restaurants.name: Pebble
restaurants.description: A chef whose skills were honed in Italian restaurants works alongside an affable owner-sommelier. Delicate, seasonal dishes are paired with wines from around the world. The food is presented with evident enthusiasm, born of a desire to convey the dedication of the producers behind it. Designed by Tadao Ando, the modern exposed-concrete space looks out over the gently flowing Okawa River.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天満3-1-2 5F

restaurants.name: Pierre
restaurants.address: 20F, InterContinental Osaka, 3-60 Ofukacho, Kita-ku, Osaka, 530-0011, Japan
restaurants.description: Freshness is number one with the chef of Pierre, so he focuses on ingredients grown and raised in Japan. The menu only lists the ingredients, encouraging guests to give their imaginations free rein. Japanese seasonings such as mustard and chilli peppers mixed with yuzu zest lend a distinctive accent. French cooking techniques are married with Japanese ingredients, which are then prepared with a light touch. Spellbinding views from the spacious dining room high in a tall building add to the ambience.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区大深町3-60 20F, InterContinental Osaka

restaurants.name: Point
restaurants.description: Originality is demonstrated here through ingredients from each season and classic techniques. Omakase set menus feature refined, beautiful arrangements, with light flavours to suit a modern sensibility. The name declares that the house is a gathering point, bringing together everyone and everything else involved in food and restaurants. The site chosen for its relaunch was where Point first started. Anticipation is building for this fresh turning point.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市福島区福島3-12-20

restaurants.name: QUINTOCANTO
restaurants.description: QUINTOCANTO is a cucina creativa, respectful of Italian tradition yet creative. The chef delves deep into Italian hometown cooking and presents his results with a contemporary feel. While offering perennial favourites such as Catalana of foie gras and spaghetti pomodoro, the restaurant is always conjuring new and creative menu items. Like every restaurant managed by the Salone Group, QUINTOCANTO strives to be a salon that provides guests with graceful service.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区中之島3-6-32

restaurants.name: Rakushin
restaurants.description: The name means ‘spirit of fun’ and also refers to the chef’s given name, Shintaro. All occupations have their joys and sorrows, but the chef maintains a spirit of fun. At the same time, he never forgets reverence for tradition. Lacquered wooden trays and dishes garnished with flowers convey each season. Self-expressive yet respectful of traditional boundaries, his goal is the evolution of Japanese cuisine. Cuisine is a mirror of the soul; joy itself.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市福島区福島1-6-27

restaurants.name: Ramen Hayato
restaurants.description: This long-serving ramen shop is loved by Japanese people for its “Three Great Ramens”: soy-sauce, salt and miso. First-time visitors are recommended to order the soy-sauce ramen, but once you learn that the Hokkaido-born chef studied in Sapporo, you might be tempted to go for the miso ramen. The soy-sauce ramen is served in a double soup of meat-based stock and seafood-based stock. “Ura-Shoyu”, the ‘hidden soy-sauce ramen’ not listed on the menu, uses meat-based stock only. If you’re having trouble deciding, start with the ‘official’ menu.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区南森町1-2-2

restaurants.name: Ramen Kuon
restaurants.address: B2F, Semba Centar Bldg Sangokan, 1-4-3 Sembachuo, Chuo-ku, Osaka, 541-0055, Japan
restaurants.description: The chef takes pride in his “Shio Soba” (salt noodles), his quest for a bowl of perfection. Taste the seaweed—sea lettuce and nori—and you can feel the rustle of sea breezes. Triple soup of fish, shellfish and chicken is prepared using Pi-Water, a water that is said to be extremely similar to water in living tissue. High hydration ramen noodles, prepared with a blend of three types of wheat flour, produces a texture similar to noodles kneaded by hand. Three types of chashu are served as well, combining pork and chicken. Bringing out the best in soup, noodles and chashu for a triple treat.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区船場中央1-4-3 B2F, Semba Centar Bldg Sangokan

restaurants.name: RiVi
restaurants.description: Drawing from experience in Italian and French cuisine as well as pastries, RiVi creates a genre-less cuisine. It is a dialogue between Japanese cuisine, the influence of the chef’s birth and upbringing, and the Western cultures he admires. As his craft is always evolving, he has no standard dishes. The theme of the first half of the prix fixe menu is a celebration of all things Osaka. The meal finishes with seasonal mixed rice because he wants his guests to taste it with the bowl in their hand. Crockery created by an artisan adds beauty to the presentation.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市西区京町堀1-16-28

restaurants.name: Rooots Nakanoshima
restaurants.description: The cooking is rooted in Italian and French recipes, yet the chef applies ingenuity in the pursuit of something new. A playful amuse-bouche inspired by takoyaki is one example. Truffle is shaved over potato gnocchi at the table, releasing its rich aroma. Rice dishes showcase the lustre of freshly cooked rice. The experience delights sight, smell and taste alike.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区中之島6-1-45

restaurants.name: Ryoriya Inaya
restaurants.description: The unique menu follows a maxim the chef learned as an apprentice: ‘Good food is easy to understand, easy to eat, and delicious’. Carefully limiting the ingredients of each dish, he combines them in clever ways to draw out their special charms. Rice dishes bring the meal to a close, chosen from an extensive list that includes white rice served at the perfect point, seasonal takikomi-gohan, and a rice bowl crowned with ginger-scented, sweet-soy simmered beef. ‘Inaya’ means ‘House of Rice’; a name filled with a love and respect appropriate for the Land of Abundant Rice.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区曽根崎新地1-10-2 7F

restaurants.name: SINAE
restaurants.description: ‘Sinae’ is an acronym for ‘simple, natural, essence’. The chef presents his cuisine with simplicity, naturally using seasonal domestic ingredients to draw out the essence of each. Cooking like this that respects their aromas and flavours requires delicate preparation. Pure-white vessels take on colour from the foods that grace them, making the white of the margins all the more beautiful. The beige keynote of the dining room enfolds the diner in warmth.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区伏見町2-4-12 2F

restaurants.name: Sakamoto Sushi
restaurants.description: With Nishikujo Station to guide them to the spot, every evening customers gather at Sakamoto Sushi, where the familiar chef greets them with a smile. The omakase menu, which begins with a snack, is astounding in variety and breadth. Fish are painstakingly prepared by methods such as marination and searing, and are paired with white sushi rice in the Kansai way. Nigirizushi are interspersed with bozushi and hand-rolled sushi to keep things interesting. The chef uses the highest-grade fish, yet the prices won’t break the bank.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市此花区西九条1-23-13

restaurants.name: Sakeya Sakana Yoshimura
restaurants.description: With its rope noren curtain and cedar-ball sign, the storefront recalls an old-time watering hole. A selection of sake is available: some best served cold, others room-temperature, and still others warm; small pours can be tried from 90 ml. Sashimi styles include kelp-cured and lightly seared preparations. No shortcuts are taken: nori is toasted over charcoal, dried bonito is shaved just before serving. If you pair jellied conger eel broth with hot sake, the heat melts the aspic, blending each element into a complete harmony. Guests enjoy conversation with the chef that flows as easily as the sake and drinking snacks.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天神橋1-13-21

restaurants.name: Sennarizushi
restaurants.description: Until the previous generation, the shop had focused on sushi deliveries, but the current owner transformed it into a sushi restaurant. He changed the sushi, too, reflecting his training in the Edomae style. Sushi is served omakase style, but the chef also picks up on guests’ preferences and chooses pieces accordingly; if the guest likes to drink, he increases the number of small dishes that go well with sake. Seafood items from Osaka Bay, such as sea bream, sand shrimp, and egg cockle, are woven into the mix. Sushi is formed using red-vinegar rice.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市東淀川区菅原5-5-13

restaurants.name: Shimmachi Adachi
restaurants.description: The chef weaves the culture of everyday Japanese life, including events such as New Year, the setsubun bean-scattering ceremony and the Doll Festival, into his creations. The practices of each event are explained on the menu, so that ancient customs may never be forgotten. Each season is honoured with its proper ingredients, as well as serving-ware decorations and arrangements based on seasonal themes. At the harvest moon, cuisine is served on lacquerware bowls decorated with images of susuki (Silvergrass) and plates bearing harvest motifs such as rabbits and the moon, and garnished with ears of susuki.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市西区新町3-8-10

restaurants.name: Shinchi Yamamoto
restaurants.description: The owner-chef focuses on the blessings of nature to bring out the unvarnished flavours of his ingredients. He starts off each meal by presenting the items that have arrived that day. In support of the producers who underpin Osaka’s food, he applies his skills to the traditional vegetables of Naniwa. Past experience at a fishmonger also enables him to source high-quality seafood. He prizes the inherent flavour of each ingredient above all, so keeps arrangements simple. Antique tableware highlights seasonal dishes.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区堂島1-1-13

restaurants.name: Shintaro
restaurants.description: The eye is drawn to the two cooks frying tempura, standing side-by-side in front of their pot. The owner-chef skilfully wreathes each piece in batter and slips it into the oil. The fry cook focuses exclusively on adjusting the flame. Working in sync, the two cooks produce tempura wrapped in a thin yet flavoursome coating. Seasoning with salt further highlights the item’s flavour. Shintaro jealously preserves the methods of its founder, striving to maintain a flavour unchanged in decades.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満2-5-5

restaurants.name: Shokudo Akari
restaurants.description: Akari shines out of a detached house in the entertainment district. The nest of criss-crossing wooden poles hung from the ceiling represent the handheld pine torches of the Oto Fire Festival in Wakayama, as well as the flame of the chef’s passion for cooking. Expect everything from familiar favourites such as gingered chicken liver, potato salad and deep-fried horse mackerel, to seasonal flavours. Naturally, this true son of Wakayama takes pride in grilling with Kishu Bincho charcoal. Every February 6, the chef returns to his native prefecture to take part in the Fire Festival.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区曽根崎新地1-6-27 2F

restaurants.name: Shuko Osaka Manpukudou
restaurants.description: A ball of cedar sprigs hangs from the eaves of this traditional detached house. Accents such as an old chest of drawers and transoms fill the air with a nostalgic mood. It’s all courtesy of the proprietor, who was once a Chinese cuisine chef. Awakened to the delicious taste of sake, he switched to Japanese food, stocking local sakes from throughout Japan. True to the ‘shuko’, or appetizers served with sake, in its name, the omakase set menu consists of numerous small dishes. Friendly service creates a pleasant mood. Fill the belly, satisfy the soul: both are ‘manpuku’.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市東成区大今里西3-4-14

restaurants.name: Shunsaiten Tsuchiya
restaurants.description: Techniques from every genre of Japanese cuisine are used for tempura; items may be pre-cooked or lightly scored and seasoned to bring out their natural flavour before frying. Keen to highlight Kansai ingredients, the chef stocks octopus, pike conger and tilefish. Osaka flourished as a centre of cotton production, so the chef cooks with a unique cottonseed oil in solidarity with producers. A beacon of Naniwa food culture to the world, with imaginative twists.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府吹田市豊津町41-4

restaurants.name: Shunten Shin
restaurants.description: For this owner-chef, the tempura must have a refined aroma and not be oily, so the batter is applied thinly and the cooking oil used is mainly sesame and corn oil. Red aubergine is not soaked with oil all the way through, to preserve its vibrant juiciness. Asparagus tips and stalks are served at different times, calculated to ensure the right firmness for each. The most famous among them is the bowl of rice topped with tempura made from egg yolk, and raw uni.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市天王寺区上汐3-8-10

restaurants.name: Shunzen Kiraku
restaurants.description: This Japanese restaurant is in the style of a Naniwa (Osaka) kappo. The procession starts with a combination platter of seafood, vegetables and sushi. The menu ranges from grilled and simmered items that hew close to the basics to offerings with a creative spin such as bisques and frites. The chef’s greatest joy is concocting dishes that connect on an emotional level: indicate a preference and he is happy to prepare your dish the way you like it. He keeps in his heart the teaching of his mentor: the true measure of a chef is the ability to tailor each dish to the taste of the diner.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区東平1-3-16

restaurants.name: Sobadokoro Toki
restaurants.description: Over the front door of this shop in Kitashinchi hangs a shimenawa, a twisted straw rope that wards off evil. Inscribed on a wooden block over the shimenawa is the phrase ‘Senkyaku Banrai’: ‘May customers arrive in the thousands and tens of thousands!’ In hopes the wish will come true, the doors are kept open until late at night. The sprawling menu ranges from appetizers that accompany sake to seasonal soba dishes. At lunchtime, soba accompanied by sushi is a nice surprise. In summer, chilled curry soba is brightly festooned with veggies. In winter, oyster soba in white miso soup warms the body from the inside. The special preparations for each season give something extra to look forward to.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区堂島1-3-4

restaurants.name: Sobakiri Arabompu
restaurants.description: ‘Arabompu’ was a favourite word of Edo-era haiku poet Kobayashi Issa. It means ‘living a free and ordinary life’. For the chef here, it means approaching his subjects, buckwheat and soba, from a natural stance. To watch his series of tasks—parboiling, washing, then plating the soba noodles—there’s no substitute for a seat at the counter. The ‘Soba Zanmai’ set offers three types of soba—nihachi, juwari, and arabiki—served as half bowls of each for tasting. Kanto-style dipping sauce recalls the days of the chef’s apprenticeship.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-1-11

restaurants.name: Sobakiri Gaku
restaurants.description: It’s counter seating only in this modern interior accented with natural materials. The chef likes his soba to have a certain translucency, so he kneads it into ni-hachi, which contains 20% wheat flour. Soba is served cold on wicker baskets, not in soup, so patrons can appreciate the fragrance and firmness of the grain. ‘Kaeshi’, a mixture of soy sauce, sugar and mirin, is used to good effect in the rich dipping sauce, displaying the chef’s Kanto training. ‘Gaku’ means ‘mountain and sky’, signifying the chef’s commitment to attain ever higher goals.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区谷町7-1-54

restaurants.name: Sobakiri Karani
restaurants.description: Sobakiri Karani worked with a woodworking artist to create the interior using re-used wooden materials. Large tables with mismatched chairs and unique ceiling paintings express a whimsical spirit. The menu’s appetisers, including tofu pickled in unrefined sake and duck-and-onion miso, tempts one to a cup of sake. Soba is coarse-ground and cut into both thin and thick noodles, furnishing variety in finish and flavours. Enjoy soba the way you like it in a congenial atmosphere.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市福島区鷺洲2-11-26

restaurants.name: Sugimachi
restaurants.description: The owner-chef pays meticulous care to ingredient combinations. He combines the bounty of sea and mountain, but also dives deep into the techniques he has studied to gain inspiration. One example is his unseasoned grilled eel and stir-fried great burdock inspired by Yawatamaki roll. Kombu kelp, dried tuna and bonito flakes dashi soup stock provide the base. Hot pots include tomatoes; chicken dashi soup stock is added to takikomi-gohan. Here, ingenious tastes are calculated to create flavours.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区安土町1-6-22

restaurants.name: Sui Okazaki
restaurants.description: Intent on breathing novelty into Japanese cuisine, the chef does not shrink from making changes. He pulls out all the stops in both creativity and skill, with innovations such as sashimi marinated in soy sauce or wrapped in nori. Dashi is drawn in an enamelled pot, on the theory that this elicits its pure umami. ‘Sui’ is a tribute to the restaurant where the chef apprenticed, whose lessons he applies in his own cuisine. Giving thanks for techniques learned while moving ever forwards defines this chef’s mission.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区東心斎橋1-16-20 2F

restaurants.name: Sui Oya
restaurants.description: ‘Sui’ means ‘green’. The chef chose the name so as not to forget where he came from, as his birth month and the day he went independent were both in the season of fresh green leaves. Having apprenticed under a Naniwa kappo mentor, the chef believes in a Japanese cuisine that thinks outside the box. Guests can choose an extra side dish, with Western‑style options such as crab cream croquettes and beef cutlet also available. Enjoy pairings with not just sake but a selection of wines as well.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天満3-1-2 2F

restaurants.name: Suigyo Murabayashi
restaurants.description: Since his apprentice days, the chef has made a habit of touring Japan to build trust with fishermen. The blessing that flows from that effort is the high-quality seafood delivered to him from each region. The first dish offered is sashimi in a large bowl. Fish of the season arrives little by little, in wondrous variety, as the sake flows nicely. Fish stew is prepared in soy sauce and water only, bringing out the natural sweetness of the flesh. The remaining broth is served with fresh-cooked rice, wasting nothing.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区堂島1-2-17 B1F

restaurants.name: Sumibi Iwata
restaurants.description: Locally raised chickens from Kagoshima are shipped directly to the restaurant and grilled over Bincho charcoal. The appetiser for the prix fixe menu is lightly seared chicken thigh. Yakitori is almost always salted, so as to bring the high quality of the meat to the fore. Yuba tofu, soy milk thickened with starch, is the house’s renowned palate cleanser. The flavour of the chicken melts into the soup of the soy-sauce ramen that brings the meal to a close. Adding oyakodon is warmly recommended. As befits the restaurant’s name, which starts with ‘sumibi’, ‘charcoal flame’, the interior is ash-grey.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市都島区都島中通3-5-8

restaurants.name: Sumisho Mikuriya
restaurants.description: The proprietor shares his extensive knowledge of yakitori with a touch of humour—occasionally warning that it might be a long story. He constantly experiments with types of charcoal, flame settings, how the fat drips, and when to turn the skewers over. Salt, not sauce, brings out the flavour of the chicken. Another cherished view is that effective flame modulation can yield impressive results even if the chicken is not top-grade; for that reason he dares to use broiler chickens for neck and heart meat. This is the path of the ‘charcoal master’.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満5-10-4

restaurants.name: Sushi Enishi
restaurants.description: The chef learned Japanese cooking at his childhood home, a restaurant with attached inn; destiny led him down the path of the sushi master. Fish is from the markets of Temma and Kizu. Dashi soup stock is drawn from kombu kelp and dried bonito flakes; the addition of vegetables stems from his experience in other disciplines of Japanese cuisine. To modulate the flavours, sushi topping temperatures are varied and the tartness of citrus is used. The service is an experience of a lifetime, even if you’re a first-time guest. The shop’s name, enishi, or ‘connection’, signals the importance the chef places on human relationships.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区黒崎町11-1

restaurants.name: Sushi Harasho
restaurants.description: The stately tea-house interior and Japanese-style painting of the spray from a waterfall enfold guests in an atmosphere of tranquillity. Proclaiming a no-nonsense approach to sushi, the two chefs restrict seasoning and other preparations to the bare minimum. They add no sugar to the sushi rice, the better to draw out the natural sweetness of fish and rice. Tuna is marinated in soy sauce only briefly; conger eel dressed in sauce that is only slightly sweet. As they form sushi by hand, both chefs focus on the basics of rice, wasabi, vinegar and fish.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市天王寺区上之宮町3-30

restaurants.name: Sushi Hoshiyama
restaurants.description: Sea bream is marinated in kombu, gizzard shad in vinegar; sushi rice is seasoned with red vinegar and salt. The scrupulous training the chef received as an apprentice pervades every aspect of the restaurant. Nigirizushi begins with pale white-flesh fish. Tuna, served three pieces in succession, leaves an impact. Sushi rice is served on the warm side, the better to bring out the sweetness of the fish oil. Simmered conger eel brings the meal to a close, as in the old days. The restaurant stands on the river’s edge, so you can watch the boats plying the waters below as you dine.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区菅原町1-16

restaurants.name: Sushi Ichiei
restaurants.description: Inheriting his father’s dream of independence, the chef followed him along the path of the sushi master. The signature item on the omakase menu is tsukuri of shrimp. The arrival of ginger wrapped in nori signals the transition from appetizers to sushi. Nigiri are orthodox, though serving shrimp raw adds a distinctive touch. The chef’s assured technique shows even in the thick rolled omelette. ‘Ichiei’ means something like ‘forever’, capturing his hope that you will treasure your encounter with his shop and remain a friend to it.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区千日前1-6-10 B1F

restaurants.name: Sushi Kyomachibori Sato
restaurants.description: Snacks and nigiri testify to the chef’s experience in Japanese cuisine. The meal begins simply, with handmade tofu or pureed vegetable soup. Tilefish scales are scored before the fish is grilled to make it look like a pinecone; pike conger and longtooth grouper are served as shabu-shabu, adding hot dishes to the mix. Sushi toppings such as Orient clams and simmered conger eel attest to old-school craftsmanship. Keen to offer a wide range of flavours, the chef offers smaller portions of nigiri enhanced with red vinegar and salt.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市西区京町堀1-8-22 B1F

restaurants.name: Sushi Marumine
restaurants.description: The husband-and-wife team have previously managed a fishmonger and a caterer. As a discerning connoisseur, the chef’s ability to source great ingredients is impressive. The menu, written in a beautifully flowing hand, is crammed with a rich miscellany of fish. Many of the patrons are regulars, freely ordering snacks and nigiri with cups of sake; they drop in for friendly conversation amid their daily lives in this intimate restaurant with its congenial atmosphere, a radio playing in the background.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天神橋6-2-20

restaurants.name: Sushi Murakami Jiro
restaurants.description: The talent of the faithful proprietor shows in his omakase. Let the knowledgeable proprietress guide you for pairings between sake and food. Nigiri begins with zuke-maguro, a classic Edomae topping. Gizzard shad is first lightly salted, enhancing the flavour. Instead of ginger pickled in sweet vinegar, tsukemono made with seasonal vegetables are served to provide variation. Adorning the storefront is a shimenawa, a rope of twisted straw, hung here with a prayer for a roaring trade. Guests from throughout Japan and around the world are warmly welcomed, and every evening bustles.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区曽根崎新地1-5-7

restaurants.name: Sushi Sanshin
restaurants.description: Situated in a quiet residential district, Sushi Sanshin is only open at lunchtime. The naturally lit interior presents an austere charm. The chef is schooled in the old ways of sushi-making, but weaves in a few tricks of his own. For example, the simmered clam nigiri is carefully prepared with precise knife work and finished with a hint of yuzu. The tamagoyaki, placed on top of the rice like a horse's saddle, conceals a touch of oboro within. Norimaki made with herbs to evoke vegetable flavours is a speciality found only here. Pursuit of the new, tempered by respect for the classics.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区内久宝寺町2-7-14

restaurants.name: Sushi Shigenaga
restaurants.description: The sign’s calligraphy was done by the proprietor himself. Sake vessels made of Satsuma cut glass reflect Kagoshima hometown pride. Fish is mainly shipped in from Kitakyushu, but some are selected at the Tsuruhashi and Kuromon markets. For vinegared rice, the house favours the sour flavours of eastern Japan to the sweeter varieties of the west. In Edo style, rice from the previous harvest is cooked relatively firm, vinegared and kept at body temperature. Sushi of fatty tuna is formed with salt for a more striking flavour. Sea-urchin portions are generous. Even the rolled omelette shows the practised hand of the skilled sushi artisan.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区曽根崎新地1-6-13 3F

restaurants.name: Sushi Takumi
restaurants.description: The sushi at Sushi Takumi is the product of personal connections times curiosity. Tuna, a must-have for nigirizushi, is sourced from the only wholesaler with whom the chef had a personal connection when he became independent. His work draws strength from a knife set he specially ordered from a knife store in his homeland of Fukui. From the same region comes a renowned Japanese mustard, paired with sushi toppings that best complement its flavour. Each piece of sushi weaves together personal connections, skill and a far-away homeland, enclosing sincere wishes.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区備後町3-1-2 3F

restaurants.name: Sushi Yuden
restaurants.description: This is how we remember it: the chef at work behind the old-time sushi counter, in the customer’s line of sight. ‘Sushi is a traditional cuisine of Japan’, says Yuya Nishimura. True to the food culture of Osaka, which prizes freshness above all, Nishimura seeks out the finest of the catch at Kuromon Market with the eyes of a seasoned connoisseur. The ‘Yu’ in ‘Yuden’ is a Chinese character meaning ‘calm’ or ‘everlasting’. Nishimura carries on the traditions, techniques and spirit of sushi with a style indelibly his own.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市天王寺区生玉前町1-27

restaurants.name: Sushidokoro Amano
restaurants.description: The movements of the owner-chef’s fingers as he works are a marvel to watch. This deftness is apparent in his side dishes and sushi. His knife technique, informed by a deep understanding of the characteristics of each fish, raises the flavour to a new level. Sushi rice is changed frequently to maintain just the right temperature, that of the human body. Rice is gathered in the palm of the hand as if capturing air. Wasabi, applied with the index finger, is apportioned just right, as is the nikiri. Formed with practiced economy of movement, each nigiri is formed perfectly for true flavour.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市天王寺区四天王寺1-13-2

restaurants.name: Sushidokoro Hirokawa
restaurants.description: When you order sake, the chef presents a box of paulownia wood containing Satsuma and Tenma kiriko cut-glass drinking cups. These craft traditions of Kagoshima and Osaka tell of where the chef was born, and where he apprenticed. Fish is selected at Tsuruhashi Market. The meal starts with snacks before proceeding to more creative preparations, attesting to his experience. Nigiri are formed small, in the hope you will sample as many different items as possible. The chef’s ready smile is another factor that keeps diners coming back.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市城東区蒲生1-8-39

restaurants.name: Sushidokoro Kaihara
restaurants.description: Omakase from a veteran of Japanese cuisine. The meal begins with grated vegetables and appetizers. Nigiri emphasises the toppings, with fish cut thick and sushi rice more modest in quantity. Smaller sushi pieces enable guests to sample a variety of fish to their hearts’ content. An ingenious twist is that the soy sauce varies by topping: shrimp is served with egg yolk steeped in soy sauce, fatty tuna with foamy soy sauce, and so on. This self-taught sushi artisan never stops learning and rewarding guests with the results of his labours.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区池田町5-4

restaurants.name: Sushidokoro Kurosugi Shinkan
restaurants.description: The focus is on à la carte out of a desire to be customers’ regular haunt. Fish is carefully prepared, with red vinegar for the sushi rice, in deference to the history of nigiri. White-fleshed fish is pressed between sheets of kombu; sweet shrimp is marinated with miso for an added twist. In response to customer requests for lighter flavours, young onion sprouts and cabbage are offered as toppings. ‘Shinkan’ in the name means, very roughly, ‘pouring my heart into every morsel of sushi’.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区曽根崎新地1-10-22 10F

restaurants.name: Sushidokoro SHIN
restaurants.description: The chef set his sights on becoming a sushi chef while still in primary school. His family was in the fishing business, so he admired the work of sushi chefs and began by imitating it at home. Restless curiosity supported him in developing a distinctive approach to the craft. For nigirizushi, he focused on techniques for resting and marinating sushi pieces. With an unerring eye for the nature of each fish type, he chooses red-vinegared or white-vinegared sushi rice depending on the fish. The menu interweaves side dishes and nigiri for variation, heightening guests’ sense of anticipation.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天神橋7-12-14

restaurants.name: Sushiroku
restaurants.description: A counter of hinoki cypress in a cosy interior. The chef is a man of few words, devoting himself to the sushi he’s forming. He uses two types of sushi rice to ensure harmony between rice and sushi toppings: rice vinegar with white-fleshed fish and red vinegar with fatty cuts, for example. Soy-marinaded tuna is wrapped in nori after it is formed, in the style of tekkamaki. The basis is Edomae sushi but with respect for Kansai culture. The inventiveness of the chef’s work makes it all the more impressive.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-12-22

restaurants.name: Sushisho Nehachi
restaurants.description: The delightfully varied omakase menu begins with a snack. Bright perilla spikes and grilled soy marinated fish show the chef’s experience. Nigiri is painstakingly crafted, sparing neither effort nor inventiveness. Clams are broiled to a pungent fragrance, sweet shrimp comes with roe marinated in soy sauce. The vinegar for the rice is coupled with roasted sesame seeds for an inviting aroma. The name means ‘sushi smile’, conveying the chef’s desire that patrons enjoy the restaurant in a relaxed mood.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市福島区福島4-1-70

restaurants.name: Sushitsune
restaurants.description: The dish of renown here is the bateira, or pressed mackerel sushi, conceived by Sushitsune’s first-generation owner. The fourth-generation chef continues this tradition, now popular throughout Osaka, in which pickled mackerel is pressed onto sushi rice in a boat-shaped wooden frame and dressed with white-sheet kombu. With deft handling, skilfully applying just the right force to unravel the sushi rice, the chef displays the fine skill of long practice. Bozushi, of horse mackerel and conger eel, and temarizushi are exemplars of Osaka food culture. This is the place to sample true Naniwa oshizushi, beloved by ‘Japan’s Kitchen’ of Osaka.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天神橋2-4-3

restaurants.name: TAVERNETTA da KITAYAMA
restaurants.description: In Italian, ‘tavernetta’ denotes a casual eatery, so the atmosphere here is informal and relaxing. Traditional Italian fare is listed on a blackboard. Tripe stewed in tomato, marlin baked in herb-seasoned breadcrumbs and mushroom pappardelle offer the flavours of a village in the north of Italy. The signature dish is beef, pork or lamb grilled in slabs. The menu changes based on supply. Servings are big enough for two, making them nice to share.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市天王寺区石ケ辻町1-6

restaurants.name: TEMPURA HANAGATAMI
restaurants.address: 5F, The Ritz-Carlton, Osaka, 2-5-25 Umeda, Kita-ku, Osaka, 530-0001, Japan
restaurants.description: Boxed sea urchins, foods from both land and sea, and tempura to start. The tempura, fried in blended oil, is light and airy. The secret is in the batter: to reduce gluten, wheat flour is chilled to below freezing before frying. One inventive dish is tempura of sea urchin and nori seaweed. Combinations are delightful, including eel grilled without seasoning then brushed with wasabi, and Orient clams dressed with pepper flowers.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区梅田2-5-25 5F, The Ritz-Carlton, Osaka

restaurants.name: Taian
restaurants.description: ‘Taian’ means ‘big hut’, and indeed this little place has a huge spirit. Recalling the apparent paradox of the tea ceremony, in which a small, spare space is made to feel boundless, the cosy and clean décor is exceptional in its modesty. The cuisine crystallises the skill and passion of Hitoshi Takahata, while the contrast between outward appearance and inner content is a reflection of his philosophy. Cuisine with depths of flavour satisfies the soul.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区島之内1-21-2

restaurants.name: Takeda
restaurants.description: Indigo-dyed norens are a traditional craft of Tokushima. Indigo blue is the keynote colour of chairs and trays as well, symbolising Tokushima and declaring the chef’s birthplace. Seafood arrives from fishermen in Naruto and Minami. Vegetables and soy sauce are also specially sourced, and the brand-name citrus fruit is a point of pride. Soba-gome jiru porridge is hometown cooking prepared with buckwheat seeds and vegetables. Be sure to try the Nyumen, made with Handa somen. In Osaka, the city of the chef’s apprenticeship, he showcases the charms of his hometown.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区釣鐘町2-1-6 2F

restaurants.name: Temmabashi Fujikawa
restaurants.description: The uniquely elegant counter of Yoshino cedar reflects the taste of the chef. Kaiseki meals showcase the chef’s original vision. In the middle stage of his prix fixe menu, the chef offers tempura, with each piece individually deep-fried. At lunchtime, appetisers delight the eye as well as the palate; in the evening, offerings increase in number, with sharpened focus on the ingredients. As the tempo of the menu gradually rises, the diversity of skills on display in the open kitchen piques interest.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天満2-2-21

restaurants.name: Tempura Fukana
restaurants.description: Helping his mother with the cooking as a little boy was the chef’s gateway into the world of Japanese cuisine. He learned the craft at a hotel, where he was entrusted with tempura; later he went independent, fulfilling his dream. The sign was written by his mother, a calligraphy instructor. The chef’s aim is to serve tempura so light, you wouldn’t know it’s deep-fried. Pieces are lightly coated and aromatic when fried. Points of pride include tempura using sea urchin and nori, as well as wagyu beef fillet wrapped in perilla leaf.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区堂島2-1-25 B1F

restaurants.name: Tempura Kozaki
restaurants.description: Tempura Kozaki expresses the seasons through ten pieces of tempura. The procession begins with tiger prawn, then alternates between vegetables and seafood. Experience in other disciplines of Japanese cuisine shines in the choice of ingredients such as deboned pike conger and Japanese mugwort gluten cake. Pieces are lightly deep-fried in cottonseed oil and served with warm tentsuyu. Frequent changes of coating show the skill of the seasoned veteran. This popular restaurant is full night after night; for the first Sunday of each month, reservations are accepted two months in advance.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市阿倍野区松崎町4-11-38

restaurants.name: Tempura Urakami
restaurants.description: In his tempura creations, Dai Urakami prizes lightness above all. Frying in light safflower oil brings the flavour of each piece to the fore. The meal begins with two tiger prawns. To ensure that the sweetness of the flesh makes an impression, fragrant prawn legs are served afterward. Tempura items such as abalone and milt are imaginatively paired with vinegared rice stuffed with sea urchin. The painting of Mount Fuji on the wall reminds Urakami always to seek greater heights.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市福島区福島5-6-3

restaurants.name: Tenboshi
restaurants.description: Tenboshi is managed by Numata, a developer of tempura restaurants. The chain has several locations, but this one is popular for its prices and casual style. Born in Amakusa, the chef gets many ingredients from his native Kumamoto, as well as other parts of Kyushu. Tempura, fried in a blended of fresh-pressed sesame and cottonseed oils, is light and airy. Providing seafood and vegetables alternately, with dipping sauces to set off each item to advantage, is one way to show off individuality.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天神橋7-10-9

restaurants.name: Tenjimbashi Aoki
restaurants.description: Scrupulous attention is paid to the ceremonial space, cuisine, serving vessels and sense of the seasons. The chef learned his guiding principles at a restaurant in Hozenji Alley. Entrusted with preparing the appetiser platters, he became schooled in the knowledge and aesthetics of Japanese cuisine. Aiming for artistic beauty in all aspects, he grows his own flowers to decorate the sukiya interior and collects seasonal serving vessels to delight guests. Technique, experience and sensibility all merge to create assortments of dishes that celebrate the seasons and their festivals.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区天神橋7-12-14

restaurants.name: Teppanyaki WAJO
restaurants.address: 12F, The St. Regis Osaka, 3-6-12 Hommachi, Chuo-ku, Osaka, 541-0053, Japan
restaurants.description: This is teppanyaki mixed with the essence of French cuisine and refined. Appetisers showcase modern creations, prepared with seasonal mood in mind. A double sauce of soy sauce and red wine transforms wagyu steak into an evolution of flavour. Overseeing the kitchen is Ryuta Izuka, the chef back when Joël Robuchon introduced teppanyaki dishes here. Enjoy the action in the kitchen, the sounds, the aromas, and the conversation.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区本町3-6-12 12F, The St. Regis Osaka

restaurants.name: Terada
restaurants.description: ‘Good old traditions and colourful, fun Japanese cuisine’ is the credo of owner-chef Shigeru Terada. He works traditional festivals or events into each of his hassun, which he serves with a ready smile. The reason he chooses contemporary plates and bowls is so diners can experience a journey through the present with the artists. Fresh vegetables arrive from his parents in Mie Prefecture, the images on the coaster are painted by his mother, and his elder brother designed the interior.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市天王寺区玉造元町2-35 2F

restaurants.name: Teruya
restaurants.description: The owner-chef teases out subtle flavours with a versatile sensibility and technique honed in Kyoto. He takes particular care when blending his dashi. Prepared with a light flavour, it draws out the character of each ingredient. Flavours of other ingredients transfer to the dashi in wanmono, steamed items and takiawase, lending depth to their taste. Preparations are simple, yet unseen tweaks make each item unique. Admire antique serving-ware and vessels fashioned by modern artists as you savour the tastes of the season.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区上汐2-1-24

restaurants.name: Tonkatsu Fujii
restaurants.description: In tonkatsu, the chef expresses the knowledge and passion for pork he cultivated in French cuisine. The blackboard-decorated interior creates a bistro vibe. Sauces feature red wine; the batter is flavoured with vanilla. Recalling his days as a high school baseball player, the chef’s cooking wear is in the style of a baseball uniform. ‘JAPAN’ is inscribed on the sleeve, signalling that this tonkatsu rookie aims to be Japan’s No. 1.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市旭区千林1-11-5

restaurants.name: Tonkatsu KATSU Hana
restaurants.description: Only name-brand pork from various regions is used so patrons can learn what truly delicious pork tastes like. The chef is always on the lookout for new pork brands, allowing diners to sample different flavours in tonkatsu every time they visit. If undecided between the tenderloin and the pork loin, the combination platter is the way to go. The restaurant is named ‘Hana’ because that is the name of the chef’s wife. Their warm and affable personalities add to the appeal, heightening satisfaction with the fare.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区北久宝寺町2-2-11

restaurants.name: Tonkatsu Minato
restaurants.description: A tonkatsu restaurant supervised by tempura emporium Shunsaiten Tsuchiya. Brand-name pork loin and fillet cuts are stocked, and a mixed serving of both cuts is popular. Pink slices of pork deep-fried at relatively cool temperatures reveal the tonkatsu artisan’s consummate skill. ‘Minato’ means ‘port’, and indeed the house is not far from the city’s wharfs – and ports represent commercial prosperity. As befits a restaurant in Osaka, which rose to prominence as a commercial hub, this eatery preaches the message of tonkatsu to guests from throughout Japan and the world.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市中央区島之内2-9-26

restaurants.name: Torisho Ishii
restaurants.description: The purely Japanese interior, with its orderly row of lacquer trays, is a setting fit for a kappo. The chef’s kaiseki experience manifests everywhere in the restaurant’s offerings. The name-brand chicken he uses is tender and richly flavoursome. Salt is used with restraint; instead, a light dipping sauce brings out the native flavour of the chicken. Breast meat fried with crumbled rice crackers and rice cooked with seasoned chicken mince are ideas that spring from Japanese cuisine. The restaurant’s name means ‘chicken artisan’, and it’s not hard to see why.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満3-11-4

restaurants.name: Tosara
restaurants.description: The book ‘Tosara no Ryori’ (‘Ten-Plate Cuisine’) became the guiding light of the chef’s life. One line in the book defines ‘cuisine’ as ‘a word that conveys boundless gratitude to the greatness of Nature for the vast bounty the Earth provides’. Deeply moved by the author’s values and perspective on life, the chef expresses the fascination of the natural world and food producers through a prix fixe menu of ten courses. Cuisine formed from his bonds with others is the tale of the chef’s journey.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満2-7-26

restaurants.name: Udondokoro Shigemi
restaurants.description: The chef’s top recommendation is the ‘hiyakake’: nothing but udon noodles in a golden-yellow broth. The dashi is Kansai-style, made with makombu seaweed for umami and finely chopped round herring and mackerel for flavour. Noodles are prepared with techniques learned in Kagawa. The cool broth brings out the supple body and elasticity of the noodles, showing how the goodness of each supports the other. The donburi bowl here brings together the udon cultures of Osaka and Kagawa.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市北区西天満4-7-7

restaurants.name: Ueroku wine
restaurants.description: A Spanish restaurant by Bordeaux vintner. Spain has two coasts, the Mediterranean and the Atlantic, and Japan is surrounded by seas on all four sides. These geographical features define the food culture and cooking on tap at Ueroku wine. The chef parlays Japan’s unique seafood into cuisine with a distinctive character. A selection of wines from many countries, including the owner’s own, Clos Leo, is available for your enjoyment.
restaurants.addressJa: 大阪府大阪市天王寺区東高津町4-15

restaurants.name: Ukitacho Ima
