The restaurant sources whole chickens of a variety that requires a long breeding period, and rests the meat for several days to concentrate the flavour. To bring out the flavour of the meat, salt is used liberally, sauce sparingly. Simple chicken soba noodles end the meal.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区六本木7-13-10

restaurants.name: YAKUMO
restaurants.description: Customers flock to Yakumo for its famous wonton ramen. Two types of wonton, one with pork infused with the tang of ginger and the other with shrimp cooked to delightful texture, each play their part in the dish’s popularity. To enjoy both, order the special ‘Tokusei’ ramen. The choice of soups is ‘White dashi’ made with white soy sauce, ‘Black dashi’ using rich, dark soy sauce, and ‘Mix’, a mixture of the two. And choose from thin noodles or flat. This flexibility to tailor your order is a special pleasure of Yakumo.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都目黒区東山3-6-15

restaurants.name: YAMATO
restaurants.description: Here the speciality is chargrilled vegetables and seafood. Guests gather around the hearth and charcoal brazier to enjoy the rising aroma of the seafood and vegetables while savouring sake. Among the favourites are the large clams and dried, salted ichiyaboshi fish. Seasonal delicacies like bamboo shoots and mushrooms are also available. The proprietress expertly handles the grilling duties, carefully tending to the charcoal fire. ‘Yamato’ is the name of her family's river fish shop, serving as the inspiration for the owner's charcoal cooking.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区日本橋富沢町16-3

restaurants.name: YAUMAY
restaurants.address: 2F, Nijubashi Square, 3-2-3 Marunouchi, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, 100-0005, Japan
restaurants.description: A specialist in dim sum, popular in cities around the world. The red exterior belies the classic dining experience within, which is lively on every visit. Enjoy the culture of dim sum with Chinese tea to your heart’s content. The full complement of authentic dishes includes jasmine-smoked pork, steamed scallop siu mai dumplings, steamed shrimp gyoza dumplings and rice-noodle rolls wrapped in fried tofu skin. If the weather’s nice, taking in the local ambience on the terrace is recommended.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都千代田区丸の内3-2-3 2F, Nijubashi Square

restaurants.name: Yaesu Unagi Hashimoto
restaurants.description: An old wooden sign catches the eye: ‘Unagi – eat this, and there is no need for medicine.’ This reflects the founder’s belief in eel as a nourishing food. The fourth-generation chef honours tradition while adapting to the present. The Unatsugu-ju carries the spirit of passing eel culture to the future, with efforts to release juvenile eels into the sea to replenish wild stocks. In step with the times, a counter seat offers a view of the craftsmen at work.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区八重洲1-5-10

restaurants.name: Yakitori Abe
restaurants.description: The omakase yakitori selections keep coming until you say ‘stop’. Tempo modulates in constant flux: flavours alternate from light to rich, and textures from firm to tender, punctuated with vegetables for variety. Sizes of pieces on skewers vary by cut of meat, accentuating the character of each. In a practice unique to Japan, the chef wears a headband fashioned from a twisted towel, his face a mask of concentration as he focuses on one skewer at a time. Putting his training of the next generation to good use, the chef runs a disciplined kitchen team.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都品川区上大崎3-3-4

restaurants.name: Yakitori Hinata
restaurants.description: The owner-chef is passionate about the locally raised chicken of the Tamba-Sasayama region. The concept of yakitori with wine suggests some intriguing pairings; particularly compatible with wine are seared chicken breast dusted with salt from Guérande and liver brushed with balsamic sauce. The appetiser is chicken-liver pâté. The restaurant’s name hearkens back to the names of areas and relatives with whom the couple shares a bond; it connotes a ray of sunshine on a Kurayamizaka, which translates to ‘darkness hill.’
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区元麻布3-12-4 2F

restaurants.name: Yakitori Honda
restaurants.description: The boundless curiosity of Chef Takuya Honda shines in his yakitori. The Hinai chicken he uses is aged to bring out the umami. Honda constantly tinkers with his fare through trial and error to elicit the best flavour from each cut. Unimpressed by the usual, narrow choice of salt or sauce, he broadens the selection to include condiments like condensed soy sauce, balsamic soy sauce, and rice vinegar with herbs. The chef similarly puts effort into recommending the best wine pairings.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区湊3-5-1

restaurants.name: Yakitori Nishiki
restaurants.description: The indirectly lit counter creates a comfortable setting, with wooden trays and a variety of tableware. The omakase includes a clear katsuo dashi soup and chawanmushi, reflecting the chef’s experience in Japanese cuisine. Yakitori is seasoned simply with salt to highlight the true flavour of the free-range chicken. Negima come with yuzu kosho, while breast is paired with plum paste. Danish with liver pâté adds variety to the progression.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都文京区湯島3-36-12

restaurants.name: Yakitori Omino
restaurants.description: The counter forms a U around the grill. Kitchen staff call out to each other as they devote themselves to their work. The chef builds his skewers with generous chunks of brand-name chicken and cooks them over high flame at close range. Salt is favoured over sauce, the better to draw out the flavour of the meat. Delightful flavour variations include whole chicken heart with ginger and chicken drumettes with a paste of yuzu zest and chilli peppers. The chef works hard to train the next generation, enriching and growing the world of yakitori.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都墨田区押上1-38-4

restaurants.name: Yakitori SANKA
restaurants.description: A former hairdresser, the chef puts his skills with scissors to good use. Chicken for yakitori is cut along the fibres, making skewers that are pleasing to the eye. Salt is applied with the precision of a hairstylist, creating a symphony of flavours that feels meticulously crafted. While favouring chicken from Kochi Prefecture, the chef chooses the producing region according to the cut. Skill and beauty, fused in yakitori—be sure to give it a try.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都新宿区矢来町64-4 2F

restaurants.name: Yakitori Takahashi
restaurants.description: A man of few words, the chef fixes his attention on his grill. He focuses on every detail of yakitori—the state of the charcoal, how the skewers are assembled and grilled—pursuing the subtleties of a cuisine. His favoured type of chicken is game fowl, prized for its supple texture and rich flavour. It is seasoned with a salt base and modulated with added chicken fat or vinegar, then wreathed in smoke as it cooks on the grill. Skewers are interspersed with chicken-based snacks such as cold chicken breast and mincemeat potato salad. A procession of dishes of lively originality.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区日本橋2-10-11 2F

restaurants.name: Yakumo Uezu
restaurants.description: In pursuit of a new expression of Japanese cuisine, the restaurant brings its own inventive ideas to the table. In the sashimi platter, each variety of seafood is paired with distinct seasonings and accompaniments. Inspired by the sauces of Western cuisine, grilled beef is served with smooth vegetable purées. The house’s credo, ‘onko-tokujitsu’, embodies a spirit of warmth, compassion and sincerity. That sincerity is evident in the way the chef approaches the work.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都目黒区八雲1-3-9

restaurants.name: Yama
restaurants.description: Chef Koichi Katsumata pours his devotion into his fruit selections, which he gathers as he calls on farms throughout Japan. Flavours variously embrace citrus fruits, mangoes, peaches, figs, and chestnuts as the seasons change. Each course offers variations in fragrance, texture and temperature, and through the incorporation of vegetables. The chef was born in Yamanashi Prefecture in the imposing presence of Mt. Fuji, hence the name of the establishment (yama means ‘mountain’). Yama demonstrates that, like the rugged Japanese islands so rich in peaks and valleys, pure sweetness can take a wide variety of forms.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区白金6-16-41

restaurants.name: Yd’or
restaurants.description: A modern sensibility breathed into the traditional techniques and recipes of French gastronomy: mackerel consommé, eel simmered in red wine, ‘Cordon Bleu’ (cheese-stuffed quail) and other creative dishes. Ingredients and techniques are reappraised and reconfigured. A key point is serving dishes fresh and hot: items are brought from the counter kitchen the moment they are complete. The chef’s passion is evident as he relates episodes from his culinary journey when explaining each dish.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区猿楽町3-9 2F

restaurants.name: Yoshoku Edoya
restaurants.description: Yoshoku Edoya traces its establishment to1954, when its founder, having studied Western cuisine, converted a Japanese confectionery shop into a restaurant – it still bears the name it had at its founding. The perennial favourite here is hamburger steak, served with a generous helping of demi-glace and topped with a fried egg. The skills with which the beef is kneaded and grilled to lock in juices are those of a master craftsman. Flavours of the good old days make perfect companions for rice. Yoshoku Edoya honors both its own culinary heritage and the traditions of Western-style Japanese cuisine.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区白金6-6-1

restaurants.name: Yotsuya Minemura
restaurants.description: Down a narrow blind alley in a corner of Arakicho known for its dining spots, a traditional lantern casts a soft glow. Inside, the intimate space offers counter seating only, where the chef demonstrates the refined techniques of kappo. The menu favours individuality over formality. With the first toast in mind, fried dishes appear early in the meal. Steamed sushi seasoned with red vinegar is paired with seasonal seafood, and house-made soba is another highlight. The generosity of each portion adds to the satisfaction.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都新宿区荒木町3-21 2F

restaurants.name: Yugetsu
restaurants.description: The menu is written in the chef’s own flowing brush hand. Each item features some touch of ingenuity, with flavours that keep the sake flowing. Sake is selected according to season. Shochu is also served maewari style — blended with water and rested before serving. Dining and drinking vessels crafted by artisans heighten the enjoyment of food and drink, as does the closely coordinated service of the owner couple. As the name Yugetsu, or ‘glaze and moon’ suggests, the joyous mixture of the inevitability of the moon’s phases and the unpredictability of glazes is what makes an izakaya the perfect place for communication.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区東日本橋3-4-1

restaurants.name: ZUPPERIA OSTERIA PITIGLIANO
restaurants.description: Pitigliano is a town the chef visited in his apprenticeship and has fond memories of. During his time in Italy, the chef was deeply impressed by the custom of ‘zuppa’, or dipping pieces of bread into soups of vegetables and beans, so the menu offers an extensive selection of soups. Honest flavours and presentations mirror the food culture of the Italian countryside, featuring simple handmade pasta and char-grilled fish and meat. Scenes of diners sharing from a single plate are a reflection of daily life in Italy.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都世田谷区祖師谷3-4-9 2F

restaurants.name: ZURRIOLA
restaurants.address: 4F, Kojun Bldg, 6-8-7 Ginza, Chuo-ku, Tokyo, 104-0061, Japan
restaurants.description: Zurriola is the name of a beach in San Sebastián, in northern Spain’s Basque Country. By comparing and categorizing various elements like the ocean-surrounded environment and the use of charcoal and firewood, the chef has come to understand their connections and discovered many affinities between Spain and Japan. In his menu, caviar is smoked over grapevines; foie gras is infused with the aroma of Pedro Ximénez grapes. One man’s sensibility expressed through contemporary cuisine.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区銀座6-8-7 4F, Kojun Bldg

restaurants.name: abysse
restaurants.description: The dim interior suggests the depths of the ocean—the abyss—or the deep green of the forest. Pursuing the theme of ‘sea and mountain’, seafood and vegetables are combined in dishes that convey the bounty of Japan’s seas. The chef first understood the appeal of seafood while staying in Marseilles. Land and sea are united by the turning seasons; the clear streams of mountain uplands become rivers below. This is the eternal cycle by which the land is replenished and the ocean is refreshed. The laws of nature expressed in cooking.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区恵比寿西1-30-12

restaurants.name: amarantos
restaurants.description: The chef’s experience as a pâtissier is put to good use as he fuses a medley of ingredients into a harmonious whole. Guests watch with rapt attention as he works his kitchen and serves each item directly. As guests tuck into their meals with expressions of delight and exclamations of ‘Delicious!’, his joy is tangible. The true pleasure of countertop French dining.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区銀座5-6-7 9F

restaurants.name: apothéose
restaurants.address: 49F, Toranomon Hills Station Tower, 2-6-2 Toranomon, Minato-ku, Tokyo, 105-5549, Japan
restaurants.description: The name of the restaurant means ‘highest compliment’ or ‘apex’. The pinnacle of cuisine, perched on the top floor of a soaring skyscraper, is the concept here. The precepts of apothéose are a trinity: respect for French culinary culture; a deep spirit of inquiry into Japanese ingredients; and devotion to knowing the present moment. Willingness to experiment, unconstrained by convention, ensures customers understand the joys of food.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区虎ノ門虎ノ門ヒルズステーションタ2-6-2 49F, Toranomon Hills Station Tower

restaurants.name: au deco
restaurants.description: With the joy of choice at its heart, this restaurant offers an à la carte selection alongside a curated list of aged wines. Foie gras is served as confit; seafood and meat dressed in sauces redolent with wine. Classical techniques are honoured. Crab baked in pastry is filled with scrambled egg to lend a note of familiarity. Classic cuisine meets aged sake in harmonious union. The very essence cultivated through the history of French gastronomy.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区恵比寿2-23-3

restaurants.name: bistro simba
restaurants.description: The concept is a ‘neo-bistro’, which was in vogue when the chef apprenticed in Paris. Casual bistro fare gets a leg up, thanks to the chef’s gastronomic skills, complimented by organic wine. The name ‘simba’ is an anagram suggesting the words simple, saveur (flavour) and simpatico. Everything is natural here—the food, the wine, the people—filling the belly and satisfying the soul.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区銀座1-27-8

restaurants.name: bonélan
restaurants.description: Tucked adjacent to its sister restaurant l’élan, Bonélan offers a relaxed dining experience, where the theme is ‘classic cuisine, casual style’. Prix fixe offerings centre on modern preparations such as vegetable terrine, seafood poêle and duck confit. Offcuts are used to make consommé, sending a message about food loss. The allure of traditional cuisine, passed through a modern filter.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区神宮前5-10-1 4F, GYRE

restaurants.name: canade
restaurants.description: The Pinocchio doll on the shelf is a souvenir from the couple’s time in Italy. The chef studied cooking there, while his wife studied wine. Macallè-style risotto is a simple farmer’s dish, made more elegant with seasonings; Tokachi beef, from cows that have calved raised for this restaurant, is grilled over straw for aroma. The restaurant’s name is from the Japanese kanaderu, ‘to play an instrument’, and indeed the couple’s cuisine and wines make a beautiful harmony.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都文京区本郷2-31-3

restaurants.name: eman
restaurants.description: Drawing inspiration from Japan’s four seasons, the chef fuses ingredients with the cuisines of various regions of Spain. His choice to take the path of Spanish cuisine was set when he tried paella as a student. Obsessed with reproducing this epiphany for his guests, the chef offers Spanish and Japanese rice cuisine in tandem. Clams on rice is a Basque recipe, arranged in Fukagawa-meshi style. A deep dive into the hometown cuisines of Spain and Japan, in the heart of Kiyosumi Shirakawa.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都江東区白河1-3-6

restaurants.name: ess.
restaurants.description: Prix fixe presentations seem to be ascendant these days, but this Italian restaurant boasts an unstinting menu of à la carte offerings. The list of pastas is so extensive that you can find a new one every time you visit, while gelato comes in plenty of flavours. Staff provide attentive service, ensuring that guests can always enjoy delicious items when they want them without hesitation. The name ‘ess.’ is short for ‘essential’, and that’s the experience this restaurant brings you.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区神泉町13-13 B1F

restaurants.name: est
restaurants.address: 39F, Four Seasons Hotel Tokyo at Otemachi, 1-2-1 Otemachi, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, 100-0004, Japan
restaurants.description: Chef Guillaume Bracaval expresses the Japanese landscape in his cuisine. Travelling across the country to familiarise himself with ingredients, he experienced its abundant nature first-hand. The map of Japan indicating where all his ingredients come from traces his journey. Wanting to create delicate and healthy dishes, he limits his use of butter and cream. Desserts inspired by contemporary art are highly original. Perched on the hotel’s top floor, the dining room affords stunning views of the city below.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都千代田区大手町1-2-1 39F, Four Seasons Hotel Tokyo at Otemachi

restaurants.name: falò
restaurants.description: The counter kitchen arrayed around a hearth suggests an open-air bonfire. The eyes are riveted to the chef as he grills the meat, throwing charcoal on the fire. The use of grilling close to the flame, grilling over an open hearth and directly placing ingredients on the charcoal, brings the ingredients and brightly burning charcoal into a tight embrace. Porchetta, patiently cooked for over an hour, is a popular item. Straw-grilled fish and foil-grilled vegetables whet curiosity and appetite. The chef works with practiced ease, laser-focused on the foodstuffs.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区代官山町14-10 B1F

restaurants.name: farsi largo!
restaurants.description: The Italian-trained chef runs this restaurant with his wife. A chance encounter with a fishmonger in Shizuoka Prefecture made the seafood of Suruga Bay the star of this show. One fish dish after another is served, from fried fish to soup and carpaccio. Panna cotta of black truffle comes from the chef’s experiences in Piedmont. The name of the restaurant means ‘blaze your own trail’, and the cuisine wordlessly conveys the chef’s single-minded passion.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区日本橋本町1-4-3 B1F

restaurants.name: fragment Azabujuban
restaurants.description: The concept is intriguing: ‘Italian cuisine from all over Japan’, the furthest thing from traditional Edo fare. Its strength is its unique routes to obtain seafood from around the country, sourced from intermediate tuna wholesalers. The chef’s speciality, tuna, is served as carpaccio, tuna salad, roast cheek of tuna and more. To encourage drop-in trade, the restaurant offers a selection of pasta at lunchtime and an extensive à la carte menu in the evening.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区麻布十番3-9-2 2F

restaurants.name: gentil H
restaurants.description: The aim here is to offer refinement through the flavour of excellent ingredients. Placing harmony of flavours above all, it focuses on temperature, fragrance and texture. The menu lists producing regions and producers, conveying gratitude for the ingredients that make the restaurant’s cuisine possible. The chef personally serves the dishes, ensuring that the recipe’s intention and passion shine through. Name-brand tea and bread baked by a friend from the same graduating class are both from the chef’s native Shizuoka.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区白金台5-18-17 2F

restaurants.name: girotondo
restaurants.description: Regional cuisine from the Emilia-Romagna region of Italy is faithfully recreated here. Especially prized are the meatballs and ragu the chef learned from a nonna during his apprenticeship. ‘Cappelletti‘, a kind of ravioli whose name means ‘little hats’, have a charming appearance and a rustic, home-style taste; enjoy them with a wine chosen by the proprietor, and it will conjure up images of an Italian dinner table.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都千代田区神田神保町1-36-1

restaurants.name: grill GRAND
restaurants.description: Beloved in Asakusa for three generations, this yoshoku restaurant treats demi-glace sauce as the soul of its kitchen. The most popular dish here is beef stew. Omurice is served with a choice of demi-glace or ketchup, a nod to the legacy shared between the former chef and his guests. The restaurant’s logo, a medieval-style shield adorned with the French tricolour, was inspired by a gift from a regular — a gesture that speaks to the warmth and goodwill of Tokyo’s traditional neighbourhoods.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都台東区浅草3-24-6

restaurants.name: hakunei
restaurants.description: The concept is a fusion of French cuisine with the bounty of Japanese foodstuffs. Meat is oven-roasted and smoked with straw; sauces are accented with bonito stock; and unripe pepper takes the place of black pepper. In the restaurant’s name, ‘haku’ suggests a pure feeling towards cooking, and ‘nei’ represents ‘meticulous’ work. Millefeuille de crêpes, a dessert crafted by patiently adding layer after layer, embodies that commitment.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区西麻布4-9-11

restaurants.name: hatsune
restaurants.description: The chef works alone in this tiny restaurant. At lunchtime the predominant theme is noodle dishes, such as dandan noodles and hot-and-sour ramen. In the evening, both à la carte and set menu dining are available. Be sure to order the appetiser platter, as it’s packed with variety and the time and effort taken to prepare it are apparent. The spring rolls, a finger food, are also recommended. The ‘Yellow Mapo Tofu with Seafood’ intrigues with its unusual name and distinctive yellow colour.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都目黒区目黒3-11-6 2F

restaurants.name: hortensia
restaurants.description: The themes here are richness and flavours. The flavour of kombu kelp is added to soup stock and other ingredients, creating culinary synergies. The chef’s approach is to focus on the foods of Japan and enhance their creativity. The cuisine weaves together the ‘three harmonies’: harmony with the changing of the seasons, harmony of flavours and gentleness of flavours. Presentation is colourful, flavours light. Traditional craftworks are used for serving vessels and cutlery, proclaiming the culture of Japan.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区新富1-5-12 2F

restaurants.name: itsuka
restaurants.description: With a name that reflects a desire to cherish ingredients, producers and the culinary craft of Chinese cuisine, ‘itsuka’ embodies a spirit of care and respect. Classic Chinese dishes are thoughtfully prepared using Japanese ingredients. The appetiser platter presents a medley from land and sea, enhanced with house-made doubanjiang and mala sauce to create a nuanced interplay of bold and delicate flavours. Sichuan cuisine is guided by the philosophy of ‘one dish, one character’ and ‘for a hundred dishes, a hundred flavours’, while Taiwanese cuisine is celebrated for drawing out the natural depth of its ingredients. Drawing on experience with both traditions, the chef presents a prix fixe menu that reveals a personal culinary journey.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区南青山2-14-15 2F

restaurants.name: jeeten
restaurants.description: The billboard says ‘Chinese home cooking, Yoshida style’ and refers to nourishing Chinese food, based on yakuzen (medicinal food). Having experienced the healing power of Chinese cuisine, the owner-chef strives to devise dishes that restore bodily vigour. Helpful comments on the menu detail the effects of each, such as how mapo tofu stimulates the appetite, or sweet and sour pork with black vinegar relieves fatigue. The Beatles and other classics play in the background, making everyone feel at home.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区西原3-2-3

restaurants.name: jiü
restaurants.description: Wood-fired French cuisine defines the theme here, a marriage of classical French technique and wood flame. Meat and fish are enveloped in fire, while vegetables are gently smoked. Even the red wine sauce for meat dishes reflects this devotion to wood fire: the aromatic vegetables used in the stock are first charred over flame. The name ‘jiü’ refers to the ‘blessed rain’ that nourishes the fields, and it is with the hope that this restaurant may gently nourish the hearts of its guests.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都江東区富岡1-1-3

restaurants.name: joujouka
restaurants.description: The restaurant’s name comes from an album recorded by a former guitarist of The Rolling Stones in the 1960s. The chef is not one to be swayed by fads, as his cooking attests. He pursues his own ideals in flavour, yet never strays far from the foundations of French cuisine. In roasting meats, vegetables and fish the chef prizes heat and aroma. No-nonsense, simple preparations make the results all the more impressive.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区虎ノ門3-22-7

restaurants.name: l'Odorante par Minoru Nakijin
restaurants.description: The chef sharpened his gastronomical senses in Grasse, a city in the south of France known as the ‘capital of perfumes’. Recalling his apprentice days, the chef named his restaurant ‘l’Odorante’, meaning ‘the pungent’ or ‘the aromatic’. Refined fragrance is the name of the culinary game here. Matching classic French techniques with Japanese ingredients, he fuses the two into a Franco-Japanese esprit, ensuring each dish is unique and brimming with flavour. He revels in explaining the background to his cuisine, leaving the memories of the culinary journey in all five senses.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区銀座7-7-19 B1F

restaurants.name: l'élan
restaurants.description: The round logo on the tabletop menu encapsulates the chef’s vision. The varying thickness of the line suggests contrasts between sharp and subtle flavours; the circle declares that the entire prix fixe menu expresses the cuisine as a whole. Sauces and cooking focus on the classic; the fascination with ingredients is a trait cultivated during the chef’s apprenticeship in France. The combination of the chef’s learned skills and passion for cuisine gives rise to a style all his own.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区神宮前5-10-1 4F, GYRE

restaurants.name: le bistrot des bleus
restaurants.address: 2F, EAT PLAY WORKS, 5-4-16 Hiro, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo, 150-0012, Japan
restaurants.description: The ‘blue’ of this ‘bistro of the blues’ refers to the blue in the French flag, which represents liberty. The young chef and sommelier enthusiastically pour out their passion for French cuisine. Home cooking from throughout France, such as choucroute and bouillabaisse, are available à la carte. Doors open at 3pm. Its location in a narrow restaurant row and the casual atmosphere within capture the bistro’s spirit of freedom.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区広尾5-4-16 2F, EAT PLAY WORKS

restaurants.name: le sputnik
restaurants.description: Creative courses channel the free spirit the chef cultivated in Paris. Venturing into new domains of French cooking, le sputnik dives deep into techniques such as such as ageing, fermenting and extracting. Dessert elements such as tuiles find their way into cooking, showcasing the chef’s patisserie experience with intricate recipes and three-dimensional arrangements. Beets with foie gras, arranged into a crimson rose, form an oeuvre of stunning beauty.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区六本木7-9-9

restaurants.name: lotus osteria
restaurants.description: Dishes learned in Piedmont and Tuscany fill the menu: the Piedmontese dish tonnato is homemade ham dressed in tuna sauce; beef stewed in red wine, a Tuscan tradition, is fragrant with black pepper. Preparations are simple, keeping the ingredients front and centre. The restaurant is located near Shinobazu Pond in Tokyo Metropolitan Ueno Park, famed for its summer lotuses, hence the name ‘lotus’.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都台東区池之端2-2-2

restaurants.name: malca
restaurants.description: The restaurant takes its name from the signboard of the chef’s grandfather’s fish shop on Awaji Island, marked with a hiragana ‘ka’ inside a circle (maru). Opening the menu reveals a wide array of dishes, each with its place of origin listed. The selection changes daily, reflecting the day’s catch and the chef’s deep connections with producers. While a set course is available, guests can also delight in the freedom of ordering à la carte, with portions adjusted to their preference.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区南青山2-27-16 B1F

restaurants.name: misola
restaurants.description: Sky blue is the colour theme of misola. Framed by azure wall, objets d’art of birds take flight. Seafood is the chef’s forte, owing to his experience in Venice. A prix fixe presentation begins with paste of salt cod, a famed local dish from Italy. Vegetables are gathered fresh each morning; meat dishes are light and delicate. Simple yet inventive, misola is the evolution of Italian cuisine.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区南青山3-10-38

restaurants.name: mærge
restaurants.description: To scale greater heights in the culinary world, Hideyuki Shibata moved his establishment to Minami-Aoyama. The shop’s name is an amalgam of marge, French for ‘margin’, ‘frame’ or ‘blank canvas’, and the English word ‘merge’. Within its margins, mærge offers a fusion of people, things and ideas from around the world. Prix fixe menus bring together two distinct styles, both honouring traditional, classic French fare and incorporating fresh, imaginative aspects. Inherited French techniques and a modern sensibility merge within this restaurant’s frame.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区南青山3-8-14

restaurants.name: nerisa
restaurants.description: The chef honed his craft at a restaurant in Italy’s Piedmont region, spending his days off exploring local trattorias and immersing himself in the region. Agnolotti follows a recipe brought back from his training. Dishes such as veal ham with tuna sauce and beef braised in Barolo reflect both Italian culinary tradition and his own experience. The chef tackles regional cuisine with restaurant-refined techniques.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都品川区小山6-7-6

restaurants.name: noura
restaurants.description: The name means ‘behind’, as in ‘behind the MICHELIN two-star restaurant out front’. In contrast to HOMMAGE, the purveyor of creative cuisine, noura is a homelike bistro that sticks to the basics. French onion soup, fish quenelle and duck confit are some of the French regional dishes on offer. Steak frîtes, a French comfort food, are a crowd-pleaser. This is French food culture for everyday life.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都台東区浅草4-10-6

restaurants.name: nôl
restaurants.address: 1F, DDD HOTEL, 2-2-1 Nihombashibakurocho, Chuo-ku, Tokyo, 103-0002, Japan
restaurants.description: ‘Harmony’ and ‘circulation’ are the keywords of this dining experience. Through his partnerships with farmers, the chef returns compost to revitalise the soil. The prix fixe presentation starts with a bowl of soup. Vegetable ends are used in the soup’s creation, expressing a wish for a society that doesn’t waste food. Prepared with French techniques, the fare is simple and light. The grey of the interior creates something of a laboratory feel.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中央区日本橋馬喰町2-2-1 1F, DDD HOTEL

restaurants.name: recte
restaurants.description: ‘Recte’ embodies the spirit of embracing things as they are. The chef uses hand-picked ingredients, prepared simply to showcase their vibrant flavours. The star of the show is the kamado cuisine, cooked over charcoal or wood in a traditional stone oven, or kamado. This method draws out the full potential of the ingredients with the power of far-infrared rays. Respect for nature, producers, and diners fuels the passion behind every dish.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区恵比寿西2-17-5 2F

restaurants.name: roku
restaurants.description: As if to echo the name, which means ‘six,’ the counter seats six. Dining this near to the chef creates a feeling of intimacy and comfort. Engaging in light-hearted conversation while watching the chef at work is magical. Variations on dishes are achieved using fermented flavourings such as koji and miso. For dessert, place yourself in the capable hands of the proprietress of the house. Wooden trays are handmade by the chef’s father; edible wild plants, picked by his mother, arrive from his native Nagano. A supportive family is a constant inspiration.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区代々木4-1-6

restaurants.name: sanka
restaurants.description: The name ‘sanka’ refers to three aromas — a trinity of ingredients, sauce and wine. Classical foundations are revisited and reinterpreted through original ideas. A signature dish showcases several varieties of potato, prepared using a range of techniques, sauces and powders to draw out their deep, earthy character. In the spirit of ‘onko-chishin’, the restaurant seeks to evolve French cuisine by learning from the past.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都中野区中野3-34-24

restaurants.name: scaglia
restaurants.description: The name means ‘fish scales’, and this restaurant is all about fish. Preparations are simple—carpaccio, soup, char-grilling—keeping the emphasis on the ingredients. Having apprenticed in Liguria, a region of Italy that faces the Mediterranean Sea, the chef shows his skill in his seafood pastas. Desserts such as tiramisu and panna cotta are traditional favourites with modern refinements.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都新宿区神楽坂6-39-1

restaurants.name: seto
restaurants.description: Named after the chef, the restaurant embodies his culinary philosophy. This culinary journey started in Italy, followed by learning avant-garde techniques in Denmark and Tokyo. From these diverse experiences, the chef creates genre-defying cuisine. Fermentation is incorporated to enrich the flavours, and oils from leaves and flowers impart a lingering fragrant finish. Situated in a basement, the dining space gives off an industrial vibe.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都千代田区九段南2-1-32

restaurants.name: sio AOYAMA
restaurants.description: When the chef speaks of ‘Italian of points,’ he means Italian cuisine cooked to just the right temperature, with just the right flame level and so on. Arrangements of pasta and risotto are simple, but with unique variations. Meat dishes may be cooked in any of three ways: grilled on an iron plate, oven-baked, or grilled over firewood. The light touch brought to cooking, guest relations and ambience, according with the mood of today’s diners, is appealing. A harmonious triangle of three points.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区北青山3-4-3 2F, Nonoaoyama

restaurants.name: there is ramen
restaurants.description: The soup accentuates the umami of the meats with the flavour of dried sardines; if you’re looking for soup with a unique and beguiling taste, you’ve found it here. Chashumen, ramen topped with roasted pork, is so covered in pork slices that they hide the noodles. Rice is available as an extra with the ramen, so try placing some of the roasted pork on top of it to make chashudon, roast-pork rice. Impressive and satisfying indeed, as if to say there is ramen here.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都杉並区天沼3-10-16

restaurants.name: unique
restaurants.description: Fine dining in a casual setting and price range is the concept. Masaaki Nakai interprets the classics to conjure a cuisine that is unique, hence the restaurant’s name. Stately presentations such as smoked terrine of foie gras and orange-fleshed yam or Asian black bear grilled in pie crust argue the case persuasively. Game such as fowl and rabbit are a seasonal speciality. Enjoy à la carte with a well-aged wine.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都目黒区目黒3-12-3

restaurants.name: wokotote
restaurants.description: The chef studied the art of kaiseki multi-course meals. In addition to omakase set meals, he serves prix fixe meals to which à la carte items can be added, so diners can enjoy Japanese cuisine as they please. Sashimi is accompanied by a dipping sauce of malted rice and fish sauce; mixed platters of drinking snacks include grilled and fried items prepared with care. The shop name is inspired by grammatical particles used to form sentences in Japanese. Cuisine, people, drinks and interior blend agreeably together, so relax and let time flow by.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都文京区根津2-35-1

restaurants.name: Äta
restaurants.description: The appetiser is a seafood charcuterie: squid and mussels dressed in aioli, finely minced raw horse mackerel, and dried chub mackerel. Be sure to try the speciality, bouillabaisse with a rich seafood flavour, and mix it rice with the leftover soup to make risotto. This is a bistro for enjoying seafood to your heart’s content, although the mains include meat dishes as well.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区猿楽町2-5

restaurants.name: Édition Koji Shimomura
restaurants.address: 1F, Roppongi T-Cube, 3-1-1 Roppongi, Minato-ku, Tokyo, 106-0032, Japan
restaurants.description: Although rooted in French techniques, the cuisine here minimises butter and cream, allowing the natural aromas and flavours of the ingredients to shine in a lighter finish. Chilled oysters with seawater and citrus jelly, as well as kadaif-wrapped john dory, have become the chef’s signature dishes. A plant-based vegan course also reflects his philosophy. ‘Edition’ speaks to his approach of constantly editing and refining recipes, always updating them.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都港区六本木3-1-1 1F, Roppongi T-Cube

restaurants.name: à table
restaurants.description: This chef built his menu through careful study of how classic recipes work. He pours his passion into such traditional dishes as pâté en croûte and pigeon pie. Blending French tradition with a modern sensibility, with a light touch on the seasoning. Also skilled in game, his wild hare royale, winner of an international competition, is available by reservation only. As dishes are served in double portions, bookings require a minimum of two guests.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都文京区湯島3-1-1

restaurants.name: çayca
restaurants.description: A once-in-a-lifetime experience, welcomed by a tea practitioner and a patissière. The meal begins with a cup of matcha, imbibed while watching a simple tea ceremony. ‘Dessert a l'assiette’, works of patisserie art on a plate, are patiently assembled one-by-one before your eyes. In the manner of French confectionary, recipes are layers of many elements. The sweetness of fruit, the tartness of citrus and vinegar, and contrasting textures are gathered on one plate. The interior, a fusion of tearoom and Western-style house, is altogether novel.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区西原3-22-15 3F

restaurants.name: Ăn Ði
restaurants.description: Modern Vietnamese food from a chef trained in French cuisine. Ăn Ði combines seasonal Japanese ingredients with bánh xèo, raw spring rolls and phở to express the four seasons of Japan with Vietnamese cuisine. With a skilled sommelier at the helm, you can also expect perfect pairings that elevate each dish. The emphasis is on wines, but the restaurant also showcases a modern approach to pairing sake and shochu. The restaurant name means '’Bon appétit!’ in Vietnamese.
restaurants.addressJa: 東京都渋谷区神宮前3-42-12
